administration.storage: Store at room temperature. Do not use if solution is cloudy or contains particulate matter.

food_instructions: Not applicable — IV administration only.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Do not freeze. Discard unused portion after opening.
lactation_note: Dextrose is a normal physiological substance; no concerns with breastfeeding.
reference_source: WHO Emergency Triage Assessment and Treatment (ETAT)

therapeutic_use: Buccal treatment of mild asymptomatic neonatal hypoglycaemia
Prevention of neonatal hypoglycaemia in at-risk infants
unlicensed_note: Widely used in neonatal units globally. Not all Indian hospitals stock commercial 40% dextrose gel — may need to be sourced or prepared extemporaneously.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 40% w/v dextrose gel; 0.5 mL/kg dose delivers 200 mg/kg glucose

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5 mL/kg buccal for mild asymptomatic hypoglycaemia; massage into buccal mucosa; feed immediately after
dosing_bands.notes: Massage gel into buccal mucosa. Follow with breastfeed or formula. Recheck blood glucose in 30 minutes.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5 mL/kg buccal massage for mild asymptomatic hypoglycaemia; recheck in 30 min; may repeat once
dosing_bands.notes: Reduces NICU admissions and promotes breastfeeding. If glucose remains low after 2 doses, escalate to IV dextrose.

hepatic_note: No specific hepatic adjustment.
contraindications: Symptomatic hypoglycaemia with seizures (requires IV dextrose)
monitoring_parameters: Blood glucose (recheck 30 min after gel application)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Only for asymptomatic or mildly symptomatic neonatal hypoglycaemia
Not a substitute for IV dextrose in severe or persistent hypoglycaemia
Ensure airway is protected during buccal application

administration.compatibility_note: Apply 0.5 mL/kg to inner cheek using gloved finger; massage for 1–2 minutes; follow with feed
administration.storage: Store at room temperature. Single-use sachets preferred.

food_instructions: Follow gel application immediately with breastfeed or formula feed.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Do not refrigerate.
lactation_note: Dextrose is physiological; no concerns.
reference_source: BMJ — Harris et al. Sugar Babies Trial

therapeutic_use: Continuous glucose infusion for persistent neonatal hypoglycaemia
unlicensed_note: Standard neonatal intensive care therapy for persistent hypoglycaemia.

formulations.indian_conc_note: D10W — 10% w/v dextrose in water; available in 100, 250, 500 mL
D12.5W for higher GIR requirement; prepared by adding D25W to D10W

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start D10W at GIR 4–6 mg/kg/min; increase by 2 mg/kg/min q30min to max 12–15 mg/kg/min; wean as feeds increase
dosing_bands.notes: GIR (mg/kg/min) = [IV rate (mL/h) × dextrose concentration (g/100 mL) × 1000] / [weight (kg) × 60]. Titrate to maintain glucose 50–110 mg/dL. If GIR >12, consider diazoxide or glucagon.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal adjustment. Monitor fluid balance in oliguric patients.

hepatic_note: No specific hepatic adjustment. Impaired gluconeogenesis in liver disease may necessitate higher GIR.
contraindications: Fluid overload (adjust concentration rather than volume)

interactions.effect: Concurrent insulin counteracts dextrose; used intentionally in GIK infusions

monitoring_parameters: Blood glucose q30min during titration, then q1–4h once stable
pediatric_specific_warnings: Central venous access required for dextrose concentrations >12.5%
Peripheral IV extravasation of >10% dextrose can cause skin necrosis
GIR >12 mg/kg/min suggests hyperinsulinism — investigate underlying cause
Abrupt discontinuation may cause rebound hypoglycaemia — wean gradually

administration.dilution: Use D10W as supplied via peripheral IV. For higher concentrations (>D12.5), use central line.
administration.infusion_rate_note: Continuous infusion via syringe pump; rate calculated to achieve target GIR
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with standard neonatal TPN additives. Avoid mixing with blood products.
administration.storage: Store at room temperature. Discard unused portion.

food_instructions: Not applicable — IV infusion. Advance enteral feeds as tolerated to wean IV glucose.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Do not freeze.
lactation_note: Physiological substance; no concerns.
reference_source: AAP Committee on Fetus and Newborn — Neonatal Hypoglycemia

therapeutic_use: Emergency seizure control in status epilepticus
unlicensed_note: Licensed for paediatric use. Rectal route widely used in pre-hospital setting. IV formulation contains propylene glycol — use with caution in neonates.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 2 mg/5 mL oral suspension; 60 mL bottle
IV solution 5 mg/mL given rectally via syringe + feeding tube; no commercial rectal tube available in India

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.2–0.3 mg/kg IV (max 5 mg) over 3 min; may repeat once after 5 min. OR 0.5 mg/kg PR (max 10 mg).
dosing_bands.notes: IV: give slowly over 3 min at max rate 0.25 mg/kg/min. Have bag-mask ready for respiratory depression. PR route preferred in pre-hospital setting.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.2–0.3 mg/kg IV (max 10 mg); or 0.5 mg/kg PR (max 20 mg). Repeat once after 5 min if needed.
dosing_bands.notes: Second-line after midazolam in many protocols. IV: slow push over 3 min. Monitor respiratory effort.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.2–0.3 mg/kg IV (max 10 mg) or 0.5 mg/kg PR (max 20 mg); repeat x1 after 5 min
dosing_bands.notes: Adult-range doses apply. Ensure airway management equipment at bedside.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5 mg/kg PR (max 10 mg) for seizure >5 min; pre-hospital rescue
dosing_bands.notes: For pre-hospital management of prolonged febrile seizures. Rectal route using IV formulation via syringe.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5 mg/kg PR (max 20 mg) for seizure >5 min
dosing_bands.notes: Single dose for pre-hospital rescue. If seizure persists, transfer to emergency department.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.1–0.3 mg/kg IV (max 10 mg) for seizures from organophosphate poisoning; repeat q5–10 min PRN
dosing_bands.notes: Adjunct to atropine and pralidoxime. Treats OP-induced seizures. May need repeated doses.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.1–0.3 mg/kg IV q1–4h PRN for spasms; or continuous infusion 5–15 mg/kg/day; titrate to spasm control
dosing_bands.notes: Continuous infusion preferred for neonatal tetanus. High cumulative doses needed. Monitor for respiratory depression and need for ventilation.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.12–0.8 mg/kg/day PO divided TID–QID for generalized spasticity; short-term use preferred; taper to discontinue
dosing_bands.notes: Short-term use preferred due to tolerance and dependence risk. Baclofen preferred for chronic spasticity.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No specific renal dose adjustment. Active metabolites may accumulate — use with caution and monitor sedation.

hepatic_note: Extensively hepatically metabolised. Reduce dose by 50% in moderate-severe hepatic impairment. Prolonged half-life expected.

interactions.effect: Concurrent use increases risk of profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, and death
Additive CNS depression; increased risk of respiratory failure; have ventilatory support available
Displaces diazepam from protein binding; increased free diazepam levels

monitoring_parameters: Respiratory rate and oxygen saturation during and after IV/PR administration
black_box_warnings: Concurrent use with opioids may result in profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, and death.
pediatric_specific_warnings: IV formulation contains propylene glycol — toxicity risk in neonates with repeated doses
Respiratory depression risk is highest in infants <6 months
Paradoxical excitation may occur in young children
No commercial rectal diazepam tube available in India — use IV formulation rectally via syringe
Neonatal withdrawal syndrome with prolonged maternal use

administration.dilution: Give undiluted. Do NOT mix with other drugs in syringe.
administration.infusion_rate_note: IV push slowly over 3–5 minutes at max rate 0.25 mg/kg/min (max 5 mg/min in older children)
administration.compatibility_note: Adsorbs to PVC tubing — administer close to IV site. Incompatible with most IV fluids.

administration.dilution: Use IV formulation undiluted via syringe attached to feeding tube or cut IV cannula
administration.compatibility_note: Insert 4–5 cm into rectum; hold buttocks together for 1–2 min
Syrup 2 mg/5 mL for accurate paediatric dosing

food_instructions: Can be taken with or without food.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light. Controlled substance — maintain security.
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk. Sedation and poor feeding in infant. Avoid or use single dose only.

therapeutic_use: Persistent neonatal hypoglycaemia due to hyperinsulinism
unlicensed_note: Licensed for hyperinsulinism in children. Proglycem (MSD) available in India on special import; limited availability.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 50 mg/mL oral suspension; chocolate-mint flavour; contains ~7.25% alcohol
100 mg capsule; can be opened and mixed with food for younger children

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5–15 mg/kg/day PO divided BID–TID; start low and titrate; response usually within 5 days
dosing_bands.notes: Assess response after 5 days at maximum dose. If no response (diazoxide-unresponsive), consider surgical management. Monitor for fluid retention and hypertrichosis.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5–15 mg/kg/day PO divided BID–TID; max 15 mg/kg/day
dosing_bands.notes: Long-term use for diffuse congenital hyperinsulinism. Monitor for hypertrichosis (45%), fluid retention (20%), neutropenia (9%).

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5–15 mg/kg/day PO divided q8h for persistent hyperinsulinaemic hypoglycaemia; assess response in 5 days
dosing_bands.notes: First-line medical therapy for congenital hyperinsulinism. Concurrent thiazide diuretic may be needed for fluid retention. Suspension contains alcohol — consider in neonates.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No specific renal adjustment. Monitor fluid balance due to sodium/water retention.

hepatic_note: Highly protein-bound and hepatically metabolised. Use lower starting dose in hepatic impairment.

interactions.effect: Synergistic hyperglycaemic effect; however, thiazides are often co-prescribed intentionally to manage fluid retention
Diazoxide may reduce phenytoin efficacy; monitor levels
Diazoxide may displace warfarin from protein binding; monitor INR

monitoring_parameters: Blood glucose (frequent initially, then at least daily during titration)
Echocardiography (pulmonary hypertension risk with long-term use)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Hypertrichosis occurs in ~45% of patients — cosmetically significant in older children
Fluid retention may cause heart failure in neonates — co-prescribe hydrochlorothiazide
Pulmonary hypertension has been reported with prolonged use in neonates
Suspension contains ~7.25% alcohol — significant exposure in neonatal doses
Limited availability in India — requires import or special access

administration.compatibility_note: Shake suspension well before each dose. Can be mixed with infant formula if needed.
administration.storage: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light. Shake well before use.

food_instructions: Give during or after feeds to improve absorption and reduce GI upset.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light. Shake suspension well.
lactation_note: Limited data. Avoid during breastfeeding.

therapeutic_use: Pain and inflammation in musculoskeletal conditions
unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=1 year. Oral use in children >=6 months in some guidelines. IM injection from age >=2 years.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 75 mg/3 mL ampoule; IM use; 25 mg/mL
Diclofenac paediatric oral drops (combination); verify single-ingredient availability

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 mg/kg/day PO divided TID (max 150 mg/day); age >=1 yr; shortest effective duration
dosing_bands.notes: Use lowest effective dose for shortest duration. GI protection with PPI if >5 days or risk factors.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 25–50 mg PO TID; max 150 mg/day; shortest effective duration
dosing_bands.notes: Approaching adult dosing. Consider topical diclofenac for localised pain.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Use with caution. Monitor renal function.
Avoid in severe renal impairment (GFR <30). Risk of further deterioration.

hepatic_note: Avoid in severe hepatic impairment. Reduced dose in mild–moderate impairment. Monitor LFTs.
contraindications: Active GI bleeding or peptic ulcer disease
Hypersensitivity to NSAIDs or aspirin (including NSAID-triggered asthma)

interactions.effect: Reduced antihypertensive effect; risk of acute kidney injury especially with dehydration
Reduced methotrexate clearance; increased toxicity risk

monitoring_parameters: Renal function (creatinine, BUN) if used >3 days
black_box_warnings: NSAIDs increase the risk of serious cardiovascular thrombotic events, including MI and stroke.
NSAIDs increase the risk of serious GI adverse events including bleeding, ulceration, and perforation.
pediatric_specific_warnings: Avoid in dehydrated children — high risk of acute kidney injury
Not recommended in children <1 year
Use shortest effective duration
IM injection not recommended in children <2 years

administration.compatibility_note: Give with food to reduce GI irritation. Enteric-coated tablets should be swallowed whole.
Deep IM injection into gluteal muscle. Max 2 days IM use.

food_instructions: Take with food or milk to reduce GI irritation.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from moisture.
lactation_note: Small amounts in breast milk. Short-term use likely compatible. Ibuprofen preferred if available.

therapeutic_use: Atrial septal defect with RV failure
Rate control in atrial fibrillation from rheumatic mitral valve disease
Symptomatic support in congenital heart defects
unlicensed_note: Licensed for paediatric use including neonates. Narrow therapeutic index — dose verification essential.

formulations.indian_brand: Digoxin Paed 50 mcg/mL (GSK), Lanoxin Paediatric Elixir
formulations.indian_conc_note: 50 mcg/mL oral solution; 60 mL bottle; contains ~10.6% ethanol
0.5 mg/2 mL (250 mcg/mL); for IV use only — not IM

dosing_bands.duration_note: Digitalisation: 20 mcg/kg total loading dose (give half, then quarter q6–8h x2). Maintenance: 5–8 mcg/kg/day divided q12h.
dosing_bands.notes: Neonates are highly sensitive. Target serum level 0.5–1.0 ng/mL. Pre-term neonates require lower doses. Check potassium before dosing.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Digitalisation: 30 mcg/kg total (half, then quarter x2 q6–8h). Maintenance: 5–10 mcg/kg/day divided q12h.
dosing_bands.notes: Infants 1–24 months require highest weight-based doses. Target level 0.5–1.0 ng/mL.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5–10 mcg/kg/day PO divided q12h; target level 0.5–1.0 ng/mL
dosing_bands.notes: For HFrEF symptom relief in CHD. Not mortality-reducing in children. Monitor levels, potassium, renal function.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5–10 mcg/kg/day PO divided q12h for symptomatic CHF with large shunt pre-surgery
dosing_bands.notes: Bridge to surgical repair. Use with diuretics and ACE inhibitors.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5–10 mcg/kg/day PO for rate control in AF or symptomatic HF
dosing_bands.notes: For rate control in atrial fibrillation complicating rheumatic mitral valve disease.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Reduce dose by 25–50%. Extend interval. Monitor digoxin levels closely.
Reduce dose by 50–75%. Consider alternate-day dosing. Frequent level monitoring.

hepatic_note: Not extensively hepatically metabolised. No specific hepatic dose adjustment.

interactions.effect: Increases digoxin levels by 70–100%; reduce digoxin dose by 50% when starting amiodarone
Hypokalaemia potentiates digoxin toxicity; monitor and supplement potassium
Increases digoxin levels; reduce digoxin dose
Increase digoxin bioavailability by altering gut flora; monitor levels
May increase digoxin levels; also interferes with some digoxin assays

monitoring_parameters: Serum digoxin level (target 0.5–1.0 ng/mL; toxic >2.0 ng/mL)
ECG (for arrhythmias, PR prolongation, ST changes)
Heart rate (hold if <60 bpm in adolescents, <80 in infants)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Narrow therapeutic index — always double-check dose calculations
Do NOT crush tablets for liquid preparations — use commercial elixir only
Use calibrated dropper for elixir dosing — NOT household teaspoon
Elixir contains ethanol (~10.6%) — significant in neonatal doses
Infants <1 year are more sensitive to digoxin effects
Hypokalaemia from concurrent diuretics greatly increases toxicity risk

administration.compatibility_note: Use paediatric elixir with calibrated dropper for doses <0.125 mg. Administer at same time daily.
administration.storage: Store at 15–30°C. Protect elixir from light.

administration.dilution: May be diluted with D5W or NS (minimum 4-fold dilution). Undiluted injection is painful.
administration.infusion_rate_note: Give IV over at least 5 minutes. Rapid IV may cause systemic vasoconstriction.
administration.compatibility_note: Do NOT give IM (pain, erratic absorption). IV dose is 75% of oral dose.

food_instructions: Can be taken with or without food. Maintain consistent diet (fibre affects absorption).
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light. Keep out of reach of children — fatal in accidental ingestion.
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk in small amounts. Compatible with breastfeeding.

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=2 years. Not recommended in children <2 years or neonates.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1.25 mg/kg PO/IV q6h (max 50 mg/dose); for motion sickness or vestibular vomiting; max 300 mg/day
dosing_bands.notes: Ages 2–5: max 75 mg/day. Ages 6–11: max 150 mg/day. Give 30 min before travel for motion sickness.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 50–100 mg PO q4–6h; max 400 mg/day
dosing_bands.notes: Adult dosing. Causes drowsiness — warn about activities requiring alertness.

hepatic_note: No specific hepatic dose adjustment. Use caution in severe hepatic impairment.
cross_reactions: Diphenhydramine (dimenhydrinate is the chlorotheophylline salt of diphenhydramine)

interactions.effect: Additive anticholinergic effects — urinary retention, dry mouth, blurred vision

pediatric_specific_warnings: Not for children under 2 years — risk of respiratory depression
Causes significant drowsiness
Paradoxical excitation possible in young children

administration.compatibility_note: Give 30 min before travel for motion sickness. Can be given with food.

administration.dilution: Dilute in 10 mL NS for slow IV push
administration.infusion_rate_note: Give slowly over 2 minutes IV

food_instructions: Can be taken with or without food.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C.
lactation_note: May inhibit lactation due to anticholinergic effects. Avoid or use short-term only.

unlicensed_note: Licensed for head lice in children >=6 months. Non-neurotoxic mechanism (physical suffocation). No resistance reported.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 4% w/w dimethicone in cyclomethicone base; 50 mL or 150 mL bottles

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply to dry hair, leave 8h or overnight, wash out; repeat in 7 days; 2 applications total
dosing_bands.notes: Apply sufficient quantity to coat all hair from roots to tips. Leave overnight (minimum 8 hours). Wash with normal shampoo. Repeat after 7 days. Cure rate 69–92%.

hepatic_note: Topical — no systemic absorption. No hepatic adjustment.
contraindications: Known allergy to dimethicone or silicones
monitoring_parameters: Check for live lice 7 days after second application
pediatric_specific_warnings: Non-toxic mechanism — no resistance issues unlike permethrin
Safe for children >=6 months
Flammable — keep away from heat sources, flames, and cigarettes during treatment

administration.compatibility_note: Apply to dry hair. Do not use hair dryer during treatment.
administration.storage: Store at room temperature. Keep away from heat and flame.

food_instructions: Not applicable — topical use only.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Flammable — keep away from open flame.
lactation_note: Topical use only; no systemic absorption expected. Safe during breastfeeding.
reference_source: Indian Journal of Dermatology — Pediculosis Update

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=2 years (oral). IV/IM from age >=1 year in hospital settings. Not for children <2 years unless directed by physician.

formulations.indian_brand: Benadryl Cough Syrup (J&J India — combination), Tuspel Syrup
formulations.indian_conc_note: 12.5 mg/5 mL diphenhydramine; Benadryl contains ammonium chloride + sodium citrate (combination) — verify plain DPH syrup

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1.25 mg/kg/dose PO q6h (max 50 mg/dose); for acute severe itch; avoid in <2 yr
dosing_bands.notes: Causes significant sedation. For acute use only. Second-generation antihistamines preferred for chronic urticaria.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1–2 mg/kg IV/IM/PO q6h (max 50 mg/dose); ADJUNCT ONLY — never delays epinephrine
dosing_bands.notes: Always give epinephrine FIRST. Diphenhydramine is adjunctive for urticaria/angioedema. Give with H2 blocker (ranitidine) for full histamine blockade.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1.25 mg/kg IV/IM stat for acute EPS; then 5 mg/kg/day PO divided QID; max 300 mg/day
dosing_bands.notes: For acute dystonic reactions from metoclopramide or antipsychotics. Response usually within 15 min IV.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No specific renal adjustment. Use with caution in renal impairment — may have prolonged effect.

hepatic_note: Hepatically metabolised. Use lower doses in hepatic impairment.
contraindications: Acute asthma (anticholinergic drying effect on secretions)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Not for use in children <2 years
Injection not for use in neonates or premature infants
Causes significant sedation — may mask deterioration in unwell children
Paradoxical excitation may occur in young children
Indian Benadryl is a combination product — not pure diphenhydramine

administration.compatibility_note: Can be taken with food. Elixir 12.5 mg/5 mL for paediatric dosing.

administration.dilution: Give undiluted or dilute in 10 mL NS
administration.infusion_rate_note: Give slowly over 3–5 minutes
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS and D5W

food_instructions: Can be taken with or without food.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light.
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk. May cause irritability or sedation in nursing infant. Avoid or use single dose.

therapeutic_use: Neutralisation of diphtheria toxin in confirmed or suspected diphtheria
unlicensed_note: Licensed for diphtheria treatment in all ages. Same dose for children and adults. Skin test mandatory before administration.

formulations.indian_brand: Diphtheria Antitoxin (VINS Bioproducts, Premium Serums & Vaccines Pvt Ltd)
formulations.indian_conc_note: 10,000 IU per vial lyophilised; reconstitute with diluent provided

dosing_bands.duration_note: 20,000–120,000 IU IV single dose based on severity: pharyngeal 20–40K; laryngeal 40–80K; bull-neck/systemic 80–120K IU. Give ASAP on clinical suspicion.
dosing_bands.notes: Same dose for children and adults — dose is NOT weight-based. Administer as soon as diphtheria is clinically suspected; do not wait for laboratory confirmation. Skin test for hypersensitivity mandatory before infusion.

hepatic_note: No hepatic adjustment.
contraindications: Known severe allergy to equine serum (proceed with desensitisation if DAT essential)
Positive skin test without desensitisation protocol
monitoring_parameters: Vital signs during and for 1 hour after infusion
Anaphylaxis monitoring — have epinephrine and resuscitation equipment ready
black_box_warnings: Equine-derived product — risk of anaphylaxis and serum sickness. Skin test mandatory. Desensitisation protocol must be available.
pediatric_specific_warnings: Dose is NOT weight-based — same as adult dose
Must perform scratch/intradermal skin test before administration
Have epinephrine, antihistamines, and corticosteroids at bedside
Serum sickness may occur 7–14 days after treatment

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute with provided diluent per vial instructions
administration.dilution: Dilute in NS and infuse over 2–4 hours
administration.infusion_rate_note: Start slowly (0.5 mL/min for 15 min), then increase if tolerated
administration.compatibility_note: Do not mix with other medications. Have anaphylaxis kit ready.
administration.storage: Store at 2–8°C. Do not freeze. Use within 4 hours of reconstitution.

food_instructions: Not applicable — IV administration.
storage_instructions: Store at 2–8°C. Protect from light.
lactation_note: No contraindication in breastfeeding mothers who require DAT.

therapeutic_use: Low cardiac output states post-cardiac surgery
unlicensed_note: Used off-label in neonates and infants. Licensed for paediatric cardiac support in children >=1 month.

formulations.indian_brand: Dobustat 250 mg/5 mL (Samarth), Dobujam 250 mg (JM Lifesciences)
formulations.indian_conc_note: 250 mg/5 mL vial (50 mg/mL); must be diluted before infusion

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2–20 mcg/kg/min IV continuous infusion; titrate to clinical response
dosing_bands.notes: Start at 5 mcg/kg/min and titrate. Hemodynamic response highly variable in neonates. Preferably via central line.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5–20 mcg/kg/min IV continuous infusion; alternative inotrope if epinephrine unavailable; max 40 mcg/kg/min in older children
dosing_bands.notes: Titrate to clinical response (heart rate, blood pressure, urine output, lactate). Tachyphylaxis may develop after 72 hours.

hepatic_note: No specific dose adjustment. Metabolised by COMT.

interactions.effect: Antagonise dobutamine effect; avoid concurrent use

pediatric_specific_warnings: Hemodynamic response highly variable between patients — titrate carefully
Extravasation can cause tissue necrosis — central line preferred
Tachyphylaxis may occur after 72 hours of continuous use
Correct hypovolaemia before starting

administration.dilution: Dilute in D5W or NS. Standard concentration: 250 mg in 250 mL = 1 mg/mL (1000 mcg/mL)
administration.infusion_rate_note: Continuous IV infusion via syringe pump. Rate (mL/h) = dose (mcg/kg/min) × weight (kg) × 60 / concentration (mcg/mL)
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with D5W, NS, and lactated Ringer's. Incompatible with alkaline solutions (NaHCO3).
administration.storage: Store at 15–30°C. Reconstituted solution stable 24h at room temperature.

food_instructions: Not applicable — IV only.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light.
lactation_note: Unknown excretion in breast milk. Short-term ICU use unlikely to affect nursing infant.

therapeutic_use: Softening and removal of impacted cerumen
unlicensed_note: Off-label use as cerumenolytic. Not widely available as pure docusate sodium ear drops in India; alternatives include Waxsol (imported) or generic cerumenolytics.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 0.5% w/v docusate sodium; 10 mL bottle with dropper

dosing_bands.duration_note: Instil 3–5 drops in affected ear, wait 15 min then irrigate; single application usually sufficient
dosing_bands.notes: Lie with affected ear up for 15 minutes after instillation. Warm drops to body temperature before use. May use for 2 consecutive nights if single application insufficient.

hepatic_note: Topical — no systemic absorption expected.
monitoring_parameters: Otoscopic examination before and after use
Confirm intact tympanic membrane before irrigation
pediatric_specific_warnings: Confirm intact tympanic membrane before use
Do not use in children with ear tubes (grommets)
Warm drops to body temperature to prevent vertigo

administration.compatibility_note: Tilt head or lie with affected ear upward. Instil drops and remain in position for 15 minutes.

food_instructions: Not applicable — ear drops.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C.
lactation_note: Topical ear use; no systemic absorption. Safe during breastfeeding.

therapeutic_use: HIV-1 infection as part of combination ART
unlicensed_note: WHO-preferred INSTI for all ages >=4 weeks and >=3 kg. Dispersible tablets available for paediatric use.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 5 mg scored dispersible tablet; dissolves in water

formulations.indian_brand: Dolutas 50 mg (Mylan), DTG 50 (Hetero), Instgra 50 (Emcure), Tivicay 50 mg (ViiV)
formulations.indian_conc_note: 50 mg film-coated tablet; for children >=20 kg and adolescents

dosing_bands.duration_note: >=4 weeks and 3–<6 kg: 5 mg PO once daily (dispersible tablets with 2 NRTIs)
dosing_bands.notes: WHO weight-band dosing: 3–<6 kg and <6 months = 5 mg/day; 3–<6 kg and >=6 months = 10 mg/day.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 6–<10 kg: 15 mg/day; 10–<14 kg: 20 mg/day (dispersible tablets, once daily with 2 NRTIs)
dosing_bands.notes: WHO weight-band dosing with dispersible tablets.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 14–<20 kg: 25 mg; 20–<25 kg: 25 mg (film-coated); 25–<30 kg: 35 mg; >=30 kg: 50 mg once daily
dosing_bands.notes: Film-coated tablets for >=20 kg; dispersible tablets for <20 kg. Exposure higher with dispersible vs film-coated tablet.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 50 mg PO once daily (adult dose) with 2 NRTIs; lifelong ART
dosing_bands.notes: Adult dosing. With strong UGT1A/CYP3A inducers, increase to 50 mg BID.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal dose adjustment. Dolutegravir increases serum creatinine by inhibiting OCT2 (not true nephrotoxicity).

hepatic_note: No adjustment for mild-moderate (Child-Pugh A/B). Not recommended in severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C).

interactions.effect: Strong UGT1A/CYP3A inducer; increase DTG to 50 mg BID

interactions.drug_or_class: Polyvalent cations (Ca, Mg, Fe, Al antacids)
interactions.effect: Chelation reduces DTG absorption; give DTG 2 hrs before or 6 hrs after
DTG increases metformin levels via OCT2 inhibition; limit metformin to 1000 mg/day

monitoring_parameters: HIV viral load (at 6 months, then every 12 months)
Serum creatinine (baseline; expected mild rise from OCT2 inhibition)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Dispersible tablets have higher bioavailability than film-coated — do not interchange dose-for-dose
Neural tube defect risk in periconception period — counsel adolescent females
Weight gain has been reported — monitor growth
Insomnia and neuropsychiatric effects may occur

administration.reconstitution: Dispersible tablets: dissolve in water; can crush and add to water or mix on spoon with small amount of water
administration.compatibility_note: Can be taken with or without food. Avoid concurrent polyvalent cation supplements.

food_instructions: Can be taken with or without food. Avoid taking with calcium, iron, or antacid supplements at the same time.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Keep in original container. Protect from moisture.
pregnancy_category: Not formally classified; periconception NTD signal — counsel appropriately
lactation_note: WHO recommends breastfeeding with ART in resource-limited settings. DTG is present in breast milk at low levels.

therapeutic_use: Vasopressor support in neonatal septic shock
unlicensed_note: Used in neonates and children. Surviving Sepsis Guidelines now prefer epinephrine/norepinephrine over dopamine in paediatric septic shock.

formulations.indian_brand: Dopamine HCl 200 mg/5 mL (TTK Healthcare), Dopamine Inj (Neon Labs)
formulations.indian_conc_note: 40 mg/mL; 5 mL ampoule (200 mg total); must be diluted before infusion

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5–20 mcg/kg/min IV continuous; alternative first-line vasopressor to epinephrine in neonatal septic shock
dosing_bands.notes: Low dose (2–5): dopaminergic (renal vasodilation); mid dose (5–10): beta-1 (inotropic); high dose (10–20): alpha-1 (vasopressor). Epinephrine now preferred per 2020 SSC guidelines.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5–20 mcg/kg/min IV for fluid-refractory hypotension post-birth
dosing_bands.notes: Ensure adequate volume resuscitation before starting. Central line preferred. Wean gradually.

hepatic_note: Metabolised by MAO and COMT. No specific adjustment.

interactions.effect: Severely prolonged and exaggerated pressor response; reduce dopamine dose to 1/10th
Concurrent IV phenytoin may cause hypotension and bradycardia

pediatric_specific_warnings: Extravasation causes tissue necrosis — treat with phentolamine infiltration
Central line preferred; peripheral IV acceptable if closely monitored
Recent guidelines prefer epinephrine over dopamine for paediatric septic shock
Correct hypovolaemia before starting vasopressors

administration.dilution: Dilute in D5W or NS. Neonatal standard: 30 mg in 50 mL D5W = 600 mcg/mL
administration.infusion_rate_note: Continuous infusion via syringe pump. Rate (mL/h) = dose (mcg/kg/min) x wt (kg) x 60 / conc (mcg/mL)
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with D5W, NS. Incompatible with NaHCO3 and alkaline solutions.
administration.storage: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light. Diluted solution stable 24h.

food_instructions: Not applicable — IV only.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light. Do not use if discoloured.
lactation_note: ICU setting — breastfeeding unlikely during acute use.

unlicensed_note: Licensed for CF patients >=5 years. Off-label in younger children aged 3 months–5 years. Limited availability in India.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2.5 mg nebulised once daily; ongoing for CF lung disease management; age >=6 years
dosing_bands.notes: Use with recommended nebuliser (jet nebuliser with compressor or PARI LC Plus). Do not mix with other medications in nebuliser. Administer before chest physiotherapy.

hepatic_note: No hepatic adjustment — inhaled with minimal systemic absorption.
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to dornase alfa or Chinese hamster ovary cell products
pediatric_specific_warnings: Use PARI BABY nebuliser with facemask for young children unable to use mouthpiece
Limited availability in India — may need import
Administer before chest physiotherapy for optimal mucus clearance

administration.dilution: Use undiluted. Do not mix with other nebuliser solutions.
administration.compatibility_note: Use with recommended jet nebuliser. Ultrasonic nebulisers may inactivate the enzyme.
administration.storage: Store at 2–8°C. Protect from light. Do not freeze. Discard if left at room temperature >24h.

food_instructions: Not applicable — inhaled.
storage_instructions: Store at 2–8°C (refrigerate). Protect from light. Do not use if cloudy or discoloured.
lactation_note: Inhaled; minimal systemic absorption. Compatible with breastfeeding.

unlicensed_note: Not licensed for neonatal use. Contains benzyl alcohol (associated with toxicity in neonates). Third-line agent after caffeine and CPAP. Very limited availability in India.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 20 mg/mL; 20 mL vial; contains benzyl alcohol 0.9% as preservative

dosing_bands.duration_note: Loading 2.5–3 mg/kg IV then 0.5–2.5 mg/kg/h continuous infusion; for caffeine-refractory apnoea; target plasma 2–5 mg/L
dosing_bands.notes: Third-line after caffeine and CPAP. Benzyl alcohol toxicity risk in preterm neonates — use with extreme caution. Monitor for seizures, jitteriness, hypertension.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Reduce dose and monitor closely. Accumulation risk in neonatal renal immaturity.

hepatic_note: Hepatically metabolised. Use lower doses. Monitor for toxicity.

interactions.effect: Additive CNS stimulation; increased seizure risk

monitoring_parameters: Serum doxapram levels if available (target 2–5 mg/L)
black_box_warnings: Contains benzyl alcohol — associated with gasping syndrome and death in neonates. Use only when benefit outweighs risk.
pediatric_specific_warnings: Benzyl alcohol preservative is toxic to neonates — gasping syndrome risk
Use only as last resort for caffeine-refractory apnoea
Very limited availability in India
Requires continuous cardiorespiratory monitoring in NICU

administration.dilution: Dilute to 1–2 mg/mL in D5W or NS for continuous infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: Continuous IV infusion via syringe pump
administration.compatibility_note: Incompatible with alkaline solutions. Precipitates with aminophylline.

food_instructions: Not applicable — IV only.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light.
lactation_note: Neonatal ICU setting — maternal breastfeeding not directly relevant.
reference_source: Cochrane — Doxapram for Apnoea of Prematurity

therapeutic_use: Various paediatric solid tumours and leukaemias
unlicensed_note: Licensed for paediatric oncology. Cumulative dose limit 550 mg/m2 (300 mg/m2 with mediastinal radiation).

formulations.indian_brand: Adriplus 10 mg (Fresenius Kabi), Doxilyd 10 mg, Duxocin 10 mg
formulations.indian_conc_note: 10 mg lyophilised powder; reconstitute with NS to 2 mg/mL

formulations.indian_brand: Adriamycin 50 mg (Pfizer), Adriplus 50 mg, Zodox 50 mg, Doxoruba 50 mg
formulations.indian_conc_note: 50 mg lyophilised powder; reconstitute with 25 mL NS = 2 mg/mL
2 mg/mL ready-to-use solution; 5 mL, 25 mL, 100 mL vials

dosing_bands.indication: Malignant neoplasm of kidney, except renal pelvis
dosing_bands.duration_note: 1.5 mg/kg IV per COG DD-4A protocol for stage III–IV favourable histology Wilms tumour; given as part of VA (vincristine + actinomycin D) + doxorubicin regimen
dosing_bands.notes: Dose per COG protocol. Lifetime cumulative limit: 550 mg/m2 (300 mg/m2 with chest radiation). Cardiotoxicity monitoring with echocardiography mandatory. Vesicant — central line required.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No formal renal dose adjustment. Primarily hepatically eliminated.

hepatic_note: Bilirubin 1.2–3 mg/dL: reduce dose 50%. Bilirubin 3.1–5 mg/dL: reduce dose 75%. Bilirubin >5: withhold.

interactions.effect: Additive cardiotoxicity; avoid concurrent use or monitor closely
Increased doxorubicin levels when given before paclitaxel

monitoring_parameters: Echocardiography (baseline and periodic — before each cycle or cumulative >200 mg/m2)
CBC with differential before each cycle
black_box_warnings: Severe myelosuppression — monitor CBC.
Cardiotoxicity — cumulative dose-dependent. Risk increased in children <5 years.
Vesicant — extravasation causes severe tissue necrosis. Treat with dexrazoxane.
Secondary malignancies (AML/MDS) may occur years after treatment.
pediatric_specific_warnings: Children <5 years at highest risk of late cardiotoxicity
Long-term cardiac monitoring required for all paediatric survivors (echocardiography annually)
Use dexrazoxane for cardioprotection if cumulative dose approaching limits
Extravasation: stop infusion immediately, aspirate, apply ice, consider dexrazoxane

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute powder with NS (10 mg in 5 mL NS = 2 mg/mL)
administration.dilution: Further dilute in 50–250 mL NS or D5W for infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: Infuse over 15–60 minutes via free-flowing IV. Continuous infusions (24–96h) may reduce cardiotoxicity.
administration.compatibility_note: Vesicant — administer via central line. Incompatible with heparin, fluorouracil, dexamethasone in same line.
administration.storage: Store at 2–8°C (powder) or 15–30°C (solution). Protect from light. Reconstituted solution stable 24h at room temperature.

food_instructions: Not applicable — IV only.
storage_instructions: Store powder at 2–8°C. Ready-to-use solution at 15–30°C. Protect from light.
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk. Contraindicated during breastfeeding.

unlicensed_note: Traditionally contraindicated <8 years due to dental staining. AAP 2024 position: short courses (<21 days) in children of any age are unlikely to cause clinically significant staining.

formulations.indian_brand: Doxt 100 mg (Dr. Reddy's), Doxybond 100 mg (Micro Labs)

formulations.indian_conc_note: 50 mg tablet for lower weight children
25 mg/5 mL suspension prepared from capsule contents in Ora-Sweet/Ora-Plus; stable 7 days refrigerated

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2.2 mg/kg PO/IV BID (max 100 mg/dose) for 7 days
dosing_bands.notes: Drug of choice for scrub typhus at any age including <8 years. AAP supports short course doxycycline in young children for rickettsial infections.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 50–100 mg PO daily–BID for 8–12 week course; >8 years and >45 kg
dosing_bands.notes: Limit duration to 8–12 weeks to reduce antibiotic resistance. Take with plenty of water; remain upright 30 min after dose.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2–4 mg/kg/day PO divided BID (max 200 mg/day); age >=8 yr; for MRSA-suspected cellulitis
dosing_bands.notes: For MRSA-suspected skin infections. Alternative: clindamycin or TMP-SMX.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2–4 mg/kg/day PO divided BID (max 200 mg/day) for 7 days; mild-moderate disease
dosing_bands.notes: For mild-moderate leptospirosis. Severe disease requires IV penicillin.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal adjustment — doxycycline is primarily hepatically/intestinally eliminated.

hepatic_note: Use with caution in severe hepatic impairment. Risk of hepatotoxicity.
contraindications: Children <8 years (relative — see AAP guidance for exceptions)

interactions.effect: Chelation reduces absorption; separate by 2–3 hours
Enzyme inducers may reduce doxycycline half-life
Increased risk of pseudotumor cerebri (intracranial hypertension); avoid concurrent use

pediatric_specific_warnings: Dental staining risk minimal with short courses (<21 days) per AAP 2024
Take upright with full glass of water — oesophageal ulceration risk
Photosensitivity — sun protection essential
Not for children <8 years except for life-threatening infections (scrub typhus, RMSF, anthrax)

administration.reconstitution: For children unable to swallow: open capsule, mix contents with small amount of food (applesauce), or prepare extemporaneous suspension
administration.compatibility_note: Take with food and full glass of water. Remain upright for 30 minutes after dose.
administration.storage: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light and moisture.

food_instructions: Take with food and a full glass of water to reduce GI upset and oesophageal ulceration risk. Avoid dairy products within 2 hours.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light and moisture. Discard expired doxycycline (degradation products are nephrotoxic).
lactation_note: CDC considers doxycycline compatible with breastfeeding. Low levels in breast milk; calcium in milk chelates drug.
reference_source: AAP Red Book 2024 — Tetracycline Use in Children

therapeutic_use: Generalised anxiety disorder in children >=7 years
unlicensed_note: FDA-approved for GAD in children and adolescents aged 7–17 years.

dosing_bands.indication: Emotional disorders with onset specific to childhood
dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 30 mg/day PO x2 weeks minimum; may increase to 60 mg/day; max 120 mg/day; age >=7 years
dosing_bands.notes: FDA-approved for GAD ages 7–17. Start 30 mg/day x2 weeks then assess. Taper when discontinuing — do not stop abruptly.

dosing_bands.indication: Emotional disorders with onset specific to childhood
dosing_bands.duration_note: 30 mg/day initial x2 weeks, then 60 mg/day; max 120 mg/day
dosing_bands.notes: Approaching adult doses. Monitor for suicidal ideation per FDA black box warning.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Avoid in severe renal impairment (GFR <30) or ESRD.

hepatic_note: Avoid in any degree of hepatic impairment. Hepatotoxicity reported.

interactions.effect: Risk of serotonin syndrome; contraindicated within 14 days
Markedly increase duloxetine levels; avoid combination

monitoring_parameters: Suicidal ideation (especially first 4 weeks and dose changes)
Anxiety symptom severity (GAD-7 or similar scale)
black_box_warnings: Antidepressants increase the risk of suicidal thinking and behaviour in children, adolescents, and young adults. Monitor closely for clinical worsening and suicidality.
pediatric_specific_warnings: FDA black box: increased suicidal ideation in paediatric patients — close monitoring required
Discontinuation syndrome with abrupt cessation — taper gradually
Monitor blood pressure (SNRI can cause hypertension)
Not approved for MDD in children (only GAD)

administration.compatibility_note: Swallow capsule whole — do not crush, chew, or open (enteric coating). Can sprinkle on applesauce if needed (do not chew beads).

food_instructions: Can be taken with or without food. Taking with food may improve tolerability.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light.
lactation_note: Present in breast milk. Potential risk of neonatal withdrawal if used in late pregnancy/breastfeeding.

unlicensed_note: FDA-approved for moderate-severe AD in patients >=6 months. For EoE >=1 year (weight-based). Limited availability and very high cost in India.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 200 mg/1.14 mL pre-filled syringe; for children 6 months–11 years based on weight
300 mg/2 mL pre-filled syringe; for adolescents and adults

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5–<15 kg: 200 mg SC Q4W; 15–<30 kg: 300 mg SC Q4W; age >=6 months
dosing_bands.notes: Weight-based dosing for children 6 months to <12 years. Given by caregiver SC.
For moderate-to-severe AD inadequately controlled with topical therapies. Continue topical emollients.

dosing_bands.duration_note: >=60 kg: 600 mg SC loading (2 x 300 mg), then 300 mg SC Q2W; <60 kg: 400 mg load then 200 mg Q2W
dosing_bands.notes: Self-administration by adolescent or caregiver after training. Rotate injection sites.

dosing_bands.duration_note: SC q2–4wk weight-based dosing; for EoE refractory to PPI/steroids, age >=1 yr
dosing_bands.notes: For eosinophilic oesophagitis refractory to PPI and swallowed topical corticosteroids.

hepatic_note: No formal hepatic adjustment (monoclonal antibody — not hepatically metabolised via CYP).

interactions.effect: Avoid live vaccines during dupilumab treatment; inactivated vaccines are safe

monitoring_parameters: Helminth screening in endemic areas before starting
pediatric_specific_warnings: Conjunctivitis and keratitis reported — ophthalmology referral if eye symptoms develop
Screen for helminth infections before starting (dupilumab may impair anti-helminth immunity)
Very high cost in India — limited access
Do not abruptly discontinue concomitant topical corticosteroids when starting dupilumab

administration.compatibility_note: Inject SC into thigh, abdomen, or upper arm. Rotate sites. Allow to reach room temperature before injection.
administration.storage: Store at 2–8°C. May be kept at room temperature (up to 25°C) for max 14 days. Protect from light.

food_instructions: Not applicable — subcutaneous injection.
storage_instructions: Store at 2–8°C. Do not freeze. Protect from light. May keep at room temperature <=25°C for up to 14 days.
lactation_note: IgG antibody — expected to be present in breast milk at low levels. Unlikely to be absorbed by infant. Limited data.

unlicensed_note: Licensed for paediatric use. Multiple biosimilar brands available in India at significantly lower cost than originator.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 50–300 IU/kg SC 3x/week; titrate to Hb 10–12 g/dL; avoid exceeding 12 g/dL
dosing_bands.notes: Start 50 IU/kg 3x/week. Increase by 25% every 4 weeks if inadequate response. Reduce by 25% if Hb rises >1 g/dL in 2 weeks. Ensure adequate iron stores (ferritin >100, TSAT >20%).

dosing_bands.duration_note: 50–300 IU/kg SC 3x/week; or 600 IU/kg IV weekly (max 40,000 IU/week); target Hb 10–12 g/dL
dosing_bands.notes: Higher IV doses for oncology setting (weekly dosing). Ensure adequate iron supplementation.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: CKD anaemia is the primary indication. Dose-titrate to Hb 10–12 g/dL. SC preferred in non-dialysis CKD; IV during haemodialysis.

hepatic_note: No hepatic dose adjustment.
contraindications: Pure red cell aplasia (PRCA) secondary to previous ESA therapy
Known hypersensitivity to EPO or mammalian cell-derived products
monitoring_parameters: Haemoglobin (every 2–4 weeks until stable, then monthly)
Iron studies (ferritin, TSAT) — replete before and during ESA therapy
Blood pressure (hypertension is common side effect)
Signs of PRCA (sudden loss of efficacy)
black_box_warnings: ESAs increase the risk of death, MI, stroke, and tumour progression in some cancer patients. Use lowest dose to avoid transfusion.
Target Hb should not exceed 12 g/dL — higher targets increase cardiovascular risk.
pediatric_specific_warnings: Hypertension common — monitor BP at each visit
Ensure adequate iron supplementation (oral or IV iron)
Do not target Hb >12 g/dL
PRCA (antibody-mediated) reported rarely — discontinue and do not switch ESAs if PRCA confirmed
Indian biosimilars significantly cheaper than originator — widely used in Indian hospitals

administration.compatibility_note: SC preferred for non-dialysis CKD (longer duration of action). Rotate injection sites.
administration.storage: Store at 2–8°C. Do not freeze. Do not shake. Allow to reach room temperature before injection.

administration.dilution: May give undiluted as IV bolus during haemodialysis
administration.infusion_rate_note: Give over 1–5 minutes IV
administration.compatibility_note: Give at end of haemodialysis session via venous blood line.

food_instructions: Not applicable — injection.
storage_instructions: Store at 2–8°C. Do not freeze. Do not shake vigorously. Protect from light.
lactation_note: Endogenous protein; unlikely to be absorbed from infant GI tract. Compatible with breastfeeding.

unlicensed_note: FDA-approved for gMG in paediatric patients >=6 years (AChR-Ab positive). Very high cost. Limited availability in India.

dosing_bands.indication: Myasthenia gravis and other myoneural disorders
dosing_bands.duration_note: Weight-based IV q2wk: 10–<20 kg 300 mg load then 300 mg q3wk; 20–<30 kg 600 mg load then 300 mg q2wk; 30–<40 kg 600 mg load then 600 mg q2wk; >=40 kg 900 mg weekly x4 then 900 mg–1200 mg q2wk
dosing_bands.notes: For AChR-Ab positive generalised MG refractory to standard therapy. Vaccinate against Neisseria meningitidis at least 2 weeks before first dose. Lifelong meningococcal risk.

hepatic_note: Monoclonal antibody — not hepatically metabolised. No adjustment needed.
contraindications: Not vaccinated against N. meningitidis (unless urgency outweighs risk with antibiotic prophylaxis)
black_box_warnings: Life-threatening and fatal meningococcal infections have occurred. Vaccinate patients with meningococcal vaccines at least 2 weeks prior to first dose. Monitor for early signs of meningococcal infection.
pediatric_specific_warnings: Meningococcal vaccination mandatory — ACWY + serogroup B
Consider antibiotic prophylaxis (penicillin V) if vaccination <2 weeks before first dose
Extremely high cost — special access or compassionate use programs
REMS program enrollment required in some markets

administration.dilution: Dilute to final concentration of 5 mg/mL in NS, D5W, or Ringer's lactate
administration.infusion_rate_note: Infuse over 1–4 hours in paediatric patients. Do not give as IV push or bolus.
administration.compatibility_note: Allow to reach room temperature before infusion. Use within 24h of dilution.
administration.storage: Store at 2–8°C. Do not freeze. Protect from light.

food_instructions: Not applicable — IV infusion.
storage_instructions: Store at 2–8°C. Do not freeze. Protect from light. Diluted solution stable 24h at 2–8°C.
lactation_note: IgG antibody likely present in breast milk. Clinical significance unknown.
reference_source: MDA — FDA Approval for Pediatric gMG

therapeutic_use: HIV-1 infection as part of combination ART
unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=3 years and >=14 kg. Dolutegravir now preferred over efavirenz per WHO 2021 guidelines.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 200 mg capsule; can be opened and sprinkled on food
600 mg film-coated tablet; for children >=40 kg and adolescents
50 mg capsule for weight-band dosing in young children

dosing_bands.duration_note: Weight-band PO once daily at bedtime: 14–<20 kg 200 mg; 20–<25 kg 250 mg; 25–<32.5 kg 300 mg; 32.5–<40 kg 350 mg; >=40 kg 600 mg
dosing_bands.notes: Give at bedtime on empty stomach to reduce CNS side effects. DTG now preferred per WHO 2021.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 600 mg PO once daily at bedtime; lifelong ART with 2 NRTIs
dosing_bands.notes: Adult dose. CNS effects (vivid dreams, dizziness) common in first 2–4 weeks — counsel patients.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal adjustment (hepatically metabolised). Not dialysable.

hepatic_note: Not recommended in moderate-severe hepatic impairment. Monitor LFTs. Risk of hepatotoxicity.

interactions.effect: Efavirenz levels reduced; increase EFV to 800 mg/day in adults >=60 kg
Efavirenz significantly reduces voriconazole levels; contraindicated at standard doses

pediatric_specific_warnings: CNS side effects common — vivid dreams, dizziness, difficulty concentrating
Neural tube defect risk — avoid in pregnancy or periconception
Give at bedtime on empty stomach
WHO now recommends DTG-based regimens over EFV-based for all ages

administration.reconstitution: Capsules can be opened and contents sprinkled on food (1–2 tsp) for children unable to swallow
administration.compatibility_note: Give on empty stomach at bedtime. Avoid high-fat meals (increase absorption and side effects).

food_instructions: Take on empty stomach at bedtime. Avoid high-fat meals within 2 hours of dosing.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C.
lactation_note: WHO recommends breastfeeding with ART in resource-limited settings. Present in breast milk.

therapeutic_use: Cystic fibrosis with at least one F508del mutation
unlicensed_note: FDA-approved for CF patients >=2 years (granule formulation) and >=6 years (tablets). Limited availability in India — imported or compassionate access.

formulations.conc_unit: mg ELX / 50 mg TEZ / 75 mg IVA
formulations.indian_conc_note: Morning tablet: ELX 100 mg / TEZ 50 mg / IVA 75 mg; take 2 tablets for >=30 kg

formulations.conc_unit: mg ELX / 25 mg TEZ / 37.5 mg IVA
formulations.indian_conc_note: Paediatric morning tablet: ELX 50 / TEZ 25 / IVA 37.5 mg; 2 tablets for <30 kg

dosing_bands.duration_note: 6–<12 yrs <30 kg: 2 paediatric AM tablets (ELX100/TEZ50/IVA75) + IVA 75 mg PM. >=30 kg or >=12 yrs: 2 adult AM tablets (ELX200/TEZ100/IVA150) + IVA 150 mg PM. With fat-containing food.
dosing_bands.notes: Take AM dose with fat-containing food (12 hours apart from PM dose). Monitor LFTs at baseline, q3 months year 1, then annually. Dramatic improvement in FEV1 and quality of life.

hepatic_note: Moderate impairment (Child-Pugh B): reduce AM dose. Severe (Child-Pugh C): not recommended.

interactions.effect: Substantially reduce CFTR modulator levels; co-administration not recommended
Increase levels; reduce Trikafta AM dose to 1 tablet every other day and PM dose q other day

monitoring_parameters: Hepatic transaminases (ALT/AST) at baseline, q3 months x1 year, then annually
pediatric_specific_warnings: Cataracts reported — ophthalmological exam recommended at baseline and follow-up
Hepatotoxicity — monitor LFTs closely
Not available as generic in India — very expensive imported therapy
Must be taken with fat-containing food for adequate absorption

administration.compatibility_note: Take AM dose (2 tablets) with fat-containing food. PM dose (IVA alone) ~12h later with fat-containing food.

food_instructions: Must be taken with fat-containing food (eggs, butter, cheese, nuts) for adequate absorption.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C.
lactation_note: Limited data. Benefits of breastfeeding likely outweigh theoretical risk.

unlicensed_note: FDA-approved for chronic ITP in children >=1 year. Approved for severe aplastic anaemia in combination with IST in children >=2 years.

formulations.indian_brand: Revolade 50 mg (Novartis), Eltromag 50 mg (Cipla), Elromo 50 mg (Zydus)

formulations.indian_conc_note: 25 mg powder for oral suspension; mix with water; cherry flavour
12.5 mg powder for oral suspension; for young children

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1–5 years: 25 mg PO daily; titrate to maintain platelets >50,000; max 75 mg/day
dosing_bands.notes: Use oral suspension for children <6 years. Titrate dose every 2 weeks based on platelet response.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 6–17 years: 50 mg PO daily; titrate to maintain platelets >50,000; max 75 mg/day
dosing_bands.notes: Adjust dose in 25 mg increments q2 weeks. Reduce dose if platelets >200,000. Discontinue if no response after 4 weeks at max dose.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2–5 yr: 2.5 mg/kg/day; 6–11 yr: 75 mg/day; >=12 yr: 150 mg/day; start day 1 with ATG x6 months
dosing_bands.notes: Used with ATG-based immunosuppressive therapy for first-line treatment of severe aplastic anaemia.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal adjustment needed. Use with caution.

hepatic_note: Reduce starting dose to 25 mg daily in hepatic impairment. Monitor LFTs closely. Hepatotoxicity risk.

interactions.effect: Chelation significantly reduces eltrombopag absorption; take on empty stomach 1h before or 2h after meals. Separate from dairy by 4h.
Eltrombopag inhibits OATP1B1; increased statin levels and myopathy risk

monitoring_parameters: CBC with differential and platelet count (weekly during dose titration, then monthly)
Hepatic transaminases (ALT, AST, bilirubin — every 2 weeks during titration)
Peripheral blood smear (monitor for morphological abnormalities)
Reticulin bone marrow staining (if available) — concern for fibrosis
black_box_warnings: Hepatotoxicity — monitor LFTs. Discontinue if ALT >3x ULN with bilirubin elevation or clinical hepatitis.
pediatric_specific_warnings: Hepatotoxicity — close LFT monitoring essential
Risk of bone marrow fibrosis with long-term use
Take on empty stomach — separate from food and dairy by 2–4 hours
Do not interchange tablets and suspension without platelet monitoring

administration.reconstitution: Oral suspension sachets: mix contents of one sachet with 20 mL water; stir vigorously; administer within 30 min
administration.compatibility_note: Take on empty stomach (1h before or 2h after meal). Separate from dairy, antacids, calcium by 4 hours.

food_instructions: Take on empty stomach at least 1 hour before or 2 hours after a meal. Do not take with dairy products, antacids, or mineral supplements within 4 hours.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Use reconstituted suspension within 30 minutes.
lactation_note: Unknown if excreted in breast milk. Avoid due to potential risk.

therapeutic_use: Macrophage activation syndrome (MAS) in sJIA refractory to glucocorticoids
unlicensed_note: FDA-approved for HLH/MAS in Still's disease (including sJIA) in neonates and older. Very limited availability in India — imported/compassionate use.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 5 mg/mL; 2 mL and 10 mL single-use vials

dosing_bands.duration_note: 6 mg/kg IV load, then 3 mg/kg q3 days x5 doses, then 3 mg/kg twice weekly; may titrate up to 10 mg/kg; for MAS in sJIA refractory to steroids
dosing_bands.notes: For MAS/HLH in sJIA refractory to glucocorticoids. TB screening mandatory before starting. Monitor for infections closely.

hepatic_note: No formal adjustment. Monoclonal antibody.

interactions.effect: Avoid live vaccines during and for >=4 weeks after treatment; IFN-gamma suppression impairs vaccine virus clearance
IFN-gamma blockade may normalise CYP450 activity suppressed by inflammation; monitor drug levels of narrow TI drugs

monitoring_parameters: Infection surveillance (TB screening before and during)
black_box_warnings: Risk of serious infections including TB reactivation, disseminated infections, and opportunistic infections due to IFN-gamma neutralisation.
pediatric_specific_warnings: TB screening mandatory before starting — treat latent TB
Serious infection risk — withhold during active infections
Very limited availability in India
Administer in setting with ability to manage infusion reactions

administration.dilution: Dilute in NS to final concentration 0.3–1.3 mg/mL. Use 0.2 micron in-line filter.
administration.infusion_rate_note: Infuse over 1 hour
administration.compatibility_note: Do not mix with other medications. Use 0.2 micron in-line filter.
administration.storage: Store at 2–8°C. Do not freeze. Protect from light.

food_instructions: Not applicable — IV infusion.
storage_instructions: Store at 2–8°C. Do not freeze. Protect from light.
lactation_note: Unknown. IgG1 antibody likely present in breast milk at low levels.
reference_source: Rheumatology Advisor — Gamifant Approval for MAS

therapeutic_use: Routine prophylaxis in severe haemophilia A (with or without inhibitors)
unlicensed_note: FDA-approved for haemophilia A prophylaxis in all ages including neonates. Available in India through Roche distributors at high cost.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Loading: 3 mg/kg SC weekly x4 weeks. Maintenance: 1.5 mg/kg weekly, or 3 mg/kg q2wk, or 6 mg/kg q4wk SC
dosing_bands.notes: Same dosing for all ages. Three maintenance options allow flexibility. Lower exposure in infants <6 months — monitor closely.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Loading: 3 mg/kg SC weekly x4 weeks. Then 1.5 mg/kg weekly OR 3 mg/kg Q2W OR 6 mg/kg Q4W; lifelong prophylaxis
dosing_bands.notes: For severe haemophilia A with or without inhibitors. Breakthrough bleeds still require factor replacement or bypassing agents.

hepatic_note: No formal hepatic adjustment. Monoclonal antibody.

interactions.effect: Thrombotic microangiopathy (TMA) reported when aPCC given concurrently with emicizumab; avoid aPCC >100 U/kg/day for >24h

monitoring_parameters: Signs of TMA (if receiving aPCC concurrently)
Anti-drug antibodies if loss of efficacy suspected
Note: APTT-based coagulation assays are unreliable during emicizumab therapy
black_box_warnings: Thrombotic microangiopathy and thromboembolism reported when aPCC (FEIBA) was administered to patients receiving emicizumab.
pediatric_specific_warnings: APTT and APTT-based clotting assays (e.g., one-stage FVIII assay) are unreliable — will appear falsely normal
Use chromogenic FVIII assay (bovine) for monitoring if needed
aPCC (FEIBA) use during emicizumab carries TMA risk — use lowest effective dose
Subcutaneous injection — train caregivers on proper technique

administration.compatibility_note: Inject SC into abdomen, upper thigh, or upper arm. Rotate sites. Allow to reach room temperature (15 min) before injection.
administration.storage: Store at 2–8°C. Do not freeze. Protect from light. May keep at room temperature <=30°C for up to 7 days.

food_instructions: Not applicable — subcutaneous injection.
storage_instructions: Store at 2–8°C. Do not freeze. Keep in original carton to protect from light.
lactation_note: IgG antibody expected in breast milk. Clinical significance unknown.

therapeutic_use: Renoprotection in chronic kidney disease with proteinuria
unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=1 month. Oral solution (1 mg/mL) can be extemporaneously compounded. Orodispersible minitablet (0.25 mg) available in some markets.

formulations.indian_brand: Enam 5 mg (Dr. Reddy's), Envas 5 mg (Cadila), Enapril 5 mg (Cipla)

formulations.indian_conc_note: 1 mg/mL suspension prepared from crushed tablets in Ora-Sweet/Ora-Plus; stable 30 days refrigerated
Enalaprilat 1.25 mg/mL injection; active metabolite for IV use

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 0.04–0.1 mg/kg/day PO once daily; titrate slowly over weeks; use oral solution for accurate dosing
dosing_bands.notes: Neonates are very sensitive. Start at lowest dose. Monitor BP closely for 4–6 hours after first dose. Risk of hypotension and renal impairment.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.08–0.5 mg/kg/day PO divided q12h; titrate to target 0.5 mg/kg/day over 2–4 weeks
dosing_bands.notes: Cornerstone of HFrEF therapy. Monitor renal function and potassium 1 week after each dose increase.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.1 mg/kg/day PO divided q12h; titrate to 0.5 mg/kg/day; max 40 mg/day
dosing_bands.notes: ACE inhibitor cornerstone of HFrEF GDMT. Monitor BP, creatinine, potassium.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.08–0.6 mg/kg/day PO daily for proteinuria and BP control; target BP <50th percentile
dosing_bands.notes: Renoprotective in proteinuric CKD. Monitor potassium (risk of hyperkalaemia). Avoid in AKI or bilateral renal artery stenosis.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.1 mg/kg/day PO divided q12h; for residual hypertension post-repair; titrate to effect
dosing_bands.notes: For residual hypertension after coarctation repair. May combine with other antihypertensives.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Standard dosing. Monitor creatinine and potassium.
Reduce starting dose by 50%. Titrate cautiously. Monitor creatinine and potassium closely.
Use with extreme caution. Start at 25% of standard dose.

hepatic_note: Enalapril is a prodrug converted to enalaprilat by hepatic esterases. Severely impaired conversion in hepatic failure — consider using enalaprilat IV instead.

interactions.effect: Risk of hyperkalaemia; monitor potassium closely
Reduced antihypertensive effect; increased risk of AKI; avoid combination
ACE inhibitors increase lithium levels; monitor lithium levels
Dual RAAS blockade increases hyperkalaemia and AKI risk; avoid combination

monitoring_parameters: Blood pressure (before and 4–6h after first dose and each increase)
Serum creatinine and BUN (baseline, 1 week after initiation/each dose change)
black_box_warnings: ACE inhibitors can cause fetal injury and death when used during 2nd and 3rd trimesters of pregnancy. Discontinue immediately if pregnancy detected.
pediatric_specific_warnings: First-dose hypotension — monitor BP for 4–6 hours after initial dose
Neonates very sensitive — use lowest dose with compounded oral solution
Renal function decline of up to 30% may be expected (functional, haemodynamic); acceptable if stable
Teratogenic — counsel adolescent females about contraception
Extemporaneous suspension requires careful preparation and shake well before each dose

administration.reconstitution: Extemporaneous suspension: crush 10 mg tablet in mortar, add 10 mL of Ora-Sweet/Ora-Plus = 1 mg/mL; stable 30 days at 2–8°C
administration.compatibility_note: Give at consistent time daily. Can be taken with or without food.
administration.storage: Tablets: 15–30°C. Compounded suspension: refrigerate; shake well.

administration.dilution: May give undiluted or dilute in up to 50 mL NS/D5W
administration.infusion_rate_note: Give IV push over 5 minutes or infuse over 5–15 minutes
administration.compatibility_note: Enalaprilat (active metabolite) for IV use. IV dose is ~50% of oral enalapril dose.

food_instructions: Can be taken with or without food.
storage_instructions: Tablets: store at 15–30°C. Compounded oral solution: refrigerate at 2–8°C; discard after 30 days.
lactation_note: Enalapril and enalaprilat present in breast milk at very low levels. AAP considers compatible with breastfeeding.

therapeutic_use: Chronic hepatitis B — severe/protracted acute HBV or fulminant hepatitis in children
unlicensed_note: Licensed for chronic HBV in children >=2 years and >=10 kg. Off-label for severe acute hepatitis B.

formulations.indian_brand: Entavir 0.5 mg (Cipla), Entehep 0.5 mg (Zydus), Cronivir 0.5 mg (Hetero)

formulations.indian_conc_note: 0.05 mg/mL (50 mcg/mL) oral solution; for children 10–30 kg

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.015 mg/kg PO once daily (max 0.5 mg); for severe/protracted acute HBV or fulminant hepatitis; use oral solution for children 10–30 kg
dosing_bands.notes: Take on empty stomach (2h after meal, 2h before next). Lamivudine-experienced patients require double dose (0.03 mg/kg, max 1 mg). Generic Indian brands are affordable.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5 mg PO once daily (1 mg if lamivudine-experienced); for >=30 kg; on empty stomach
dosing_bands.notes: Adult dosing for adolescents >=30 kg. Monitor HBV DNA levels.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Reduce dose by 50% or use 0.25 mg once daily.
0.15 mg once daily or 0.5 mg every 72 hours.
0.05 mg once daily or 0.5 mg every 7 days. Supplement after haemodialysis.

hepatic_note: No hepatic dose adjustment. Drug is renally eliminated.

interactions.effect: May increase entecavir levels due to reduced renal clearance

black_box_warnings: Severe acute exacerbation of hepatitis B has been reported after discontinuation. Monitor hepatic function for at least several months after stopping.
Lactic acidosis and severe hepatomegaly with steatosis reported (rare).
pediatric_specific_warnings: Not for children <2 years
Take on empty stomach for adequate absorption
Use oral solution with calibrated dosing spoon for children 10–30 kg
Monitor closely for HBV flare after discontinuation
Indian generic brands are widely available and affordable

administration.compatibility_note: Take on empty stomach (at least 2 hours after a meal and 2 hours before next meal). Use calibrated dosing spoon for oral solution.

food_instructions: Take on empty stomach: at least 2 hours after a meal and 2 hours before the next meal.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Oral solution at 15–30°C after opening — use within 30 days.
lactation_note: Unknown if excreted in breast milk. Avoid breastfeeding during treatment.

unlicensed_note: Licensed for all paediatric ages including neonates. Essential medicine per WHO. Auto-injectors have limited availability and are expensive in India.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 1:1000 (1 mg/mL); 1 mL ampoule; for IM use in anaphylaxis

formulations.indian_brand: Diluted from 1:1000 ampoule (1 mL in 9 mL NS)
formulations.indian_conc_note: 1:10,000 (0.1 mg/mL); prepared by diluting 1:1000; for IV/IO in cardiac arrest and neonatal resuscitation

formulations.indian_brand: EpiPen Jr 0.15 mg (imported — limited availability India)

formulations.indian_conc_note: 0.3 mg auto-injector for children >25 kg and adults

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.01 mg/kg IM anterolateral thigh (1:1000); max 0.15 mg; repeat q5–15 min PRN
dosing_bands.notes: FIRST-LINE for anaphylaxis. IM into anterolateral thigh. Auto-injector 0.15 mg for 10–25 kg. Do not delay.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.01 mg/kg IM (1:1000) max 0.3 mg; auto-injector: <30 kg 0.15 mg / >=30 kg 0.3 mg; repeat q5–15 min
dosing_bands.notes: IM anterolateral thigh. Do not give IV for anaphylaxis unless refractory to IM. Prescribe auto-injector for discharge.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.01 mg/kg (0.1 mL/kg of 1:10,000) IV/IO q3–5 min during cardiac arrest; max 1 mg/dose
dosing_bands.notes: Per PALS protocol. Give IV/IO. Do not use 1:1000 IV push — must dilute to 1:10,000.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.01–0.03 mg/kg IV (1:10,000) via UVC if HR <60 after 60s of chest compressions + PPV; repeat q3–5 min
dosing_bands.notes: NRP guidelines. IV (UVC) preferred route. ETT route (0.05–0.1 mg/kg) only if IV access delayed.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.05–0.3 mcg/kg/min IV continuous infusion for fluid-refractory septic shock
dosing_bands.notes: First-line vasopressor per SSC 2020 guidelines. Preferred in cold shock phenotype.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.01 mg/kg IM (1:1000, max 0.5 mg) q20 min x3; or IV infusion 0.1–1 mcg/kg/min for severe refractory bronchospasm
dosing_bands.notes: IM for severe bronchospasm not responding to inhaled beta-agonists. IV infusion for ICU-level status asthmaticus.

hepatic_note: Rapidly metabolised by MAO and COMT. No dose adjustment needed.
contraindications: None absolute in life-threatening anaphylaxis or cardiac arrest (benefit always outweighs risk)

interactions.effect: May cause severe hypertension and reflex bradycardia; may require higher epinephrine doses in anaphylaxis
Potentiated pressor response; use lower doses of epinephrine

monitoring_parameters: Heart rate and rhythm (continuous during IV infusion)
pediatric_specific_warnings: NEVER withhold epinephrine in anaphylaxis — no absolute contraindications
Auto-injectors have limited availability in India — train families on ampoule + syringe technique
1:1000 for IM only; 1:10,000 for IV — accidental IV of 1:1000 can be fatal
Extravasation of IV epinephrine causes tissue necrosis — treat with phentolamine
Light-sensitive — inspect ampoule before use (should be clear; discard if brown/pink)

administration.dilution: Use 1:1000 (1 mg/mL) undiluted for IM injection
administration.compatibility_note: Inject into anterolateral thigh (vastus lateralis). Avoid deltoid in young children.
administration.storage: Store at 15–25°C. Protect from light. Do not refrigerate.

administration.dilution: Cardiac arrest: dilute 1:1000 to 1:10,000 (1 mg in 10 mL NS). Infusion: 1 mg in 250 mL D5W = 4 mcg/mL
administration.infusion_rate_note: Cardiac arrest: IV push q3–5 min. Infusion: titrate 0.05–0.3 mcg/kg/min via syringe pump
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS, D5W. Incompatible with alkaline solutions. Use central line for infusion.

food_instructions: Not applicable — injection.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–25°C. Protect from light. Do not refrigerate or freeze. Discard if solution is discoloured.
lactation_note: Rapidly metabolised. Single IM dose for anaphylaxis is compatible with breastfeeding.

therapeutic_use: Severe pulmonary arterial hypertension (WHO FC III–IV)
Refractory persistent pulmonary hypertension of newborn
unlicensed_note: Not formally licensed for paediatric use. Used off-label in children with severe PAH. Limited availability in India — imported.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 0.5 mg and 1.5 mg vials; reconstitute with manufacturer-supplied diluent

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 1–2 ng/kg/min IV continuous; titrate by 1–2 ng/kg/min q15 min; maintenance typically 20–40 ng/kg/min; for severe PAH WHO FC III–IV
dosing_bands.notes: Continuous IV infusion via portable pump through tunnelled central venous catheter. Must NEVER be abruptly discontinued (fatal rebound PAH). Prepare fresh solution every 24h.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1–20 ng/kg/min IV for refractory PPHN not responding to iNO
dosing_bands.notes: For PPHN refractory to inhaled nitric oxide. NICU setting with invasive monitoring.

hepatic_note: Short half-life (3–5 min). No dose adjustment needed.

interactions.effect: Additive bleeding risk — epoprostenol has antiplatelet activity

monitoring_parameters: Continuous haemodynamic monitoring (arterial BP, CVP, PAP)
black_box_warnings: Abrupt withdrawal or sudden dose reduction may cause fatal rebound pulmonary hypertension. Always have backup pump and infusion ready.
pediatric_specific_warnings: NEVER abruptly discontinue — fatal rebound PAH crisis
Requires permanent central venous catheter — high line infection risk in children
Solution unstable — must be reconstituted fresh daily
Very limited availability in India
Families must be trained in pump operation and troubleshooting

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute with manufacturer-supplied sterile diluent. Veletri reconstituted in NS stable 48h at room temperature.
administration.dilution: Further dilute to desired concentration for portable infusion pump
administration.infusion_rate_note: Continuous IV infusion via ambulatory infusion pump. Start low, titrate slowly.
administration.compatibility_note: Do not mix with other medications. Use dedicated lumen of central line.
administration.storage: Store unreconstituted vials at 15–25°C. Reconstituted Veletri stable 48h at room temp; Flolan stable 8h at room temp.

food_instructions: Not applicable — IV infusion.
storage_instructions: Store unreconstituted at 15–25°C. Reconstituted solution stability varies by brand (8h–48h). Use ice packs for classic formulation.
lactation_note: Unknown. Short half-life suggests minimal exposure in breast milk.

therapeutic_use: Passive immunisation for rabies post-exposure prophylaxis (category III bites)
unlicensed_note: Licensed for rabies PEP in all ages. Same dose per kg for children and adults. More affordable than HRIG in India.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 40 IU/kg single dose; infiltrate maximum possible into and around wound; remainder IM at distant site from vaccine
dosing_bands.notes: Perform intradermal sensitivity test before administration (risk of anaphylaxis from equine protein). Maximum infiltration into wound. Give on Day 0 with first vaccine dose. Do NOT give if >7 days since first vaccine dose.

hepatic_note: No hepatic adjustment.
contraindications: Known severe allergy to equine serum (relative — desensitisation if HRIG unavailable and rabies exposure confirmed)
monitoring_parameters: Vital signs during and 1 hour after administration
black_box_warnings: Equine-derived — risk of anaphylaxis and serum sickness. Skin test mandatory. Have epinephrine and resuscitation equipment available.
pediatric_specific_warnings: Intradermal skin test before administration is mandatory
Have epinephrine, antihistamines, and steroids at bedside
Serum sickness may occur 7–14 days later
More affordable than HRIG (~US$20 vs US$200+ for 60 kg patient)
Do not inject RIG into same site as vaccine

administration.dilution: May dilute with NS if volume insufficient to infiltrate all wounds
administration.compatibility_note: Infiltrate maximum volume into wound bed. Remaining dose IM into anterolateral thigh (distant from vaccine site).

food_instructions: Not applicable — injection.
storage_instructions: Store at 2–8°C. Do not freeze. Use immediately after opening.
lactation_note: Rabies PEP should not be withheld due to breastfeeding.

unlicensed_note: Licensed for paediatric use. Cholecalciferol (D3) generally preferred over ergocalciferol (D2) for paediatric vitamin D deficiency in India. Both are used.

formulations.indian_brand: Ergocalciferol oral drops (limited Indian availability — often D3 used instead)
formulations.indian_conc_note: 400 IU/mL or 8000 IU/mL depending on product

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1–12 years: 2000–4000 IU/day PO for 6–8 weeks (or 50,000 IU weekly for older children); then maintenance 600–1000 IU/day
dosing_bands.notes: Verify 25(OH)D level before treatment. Recheck at 8–12 weeks. Maintenance 600 IU/day per AAP/Endocrine Society.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 50,000 IU PO once weekly x 6–8 weeks; then maintenance 600–1000 IU/day
dosing_bands.notes: Weekly 50,000 IU capsule regimen improves compliance. Recheck 25(OH)D at 8 weeks.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: In CKD stages 4–5, use active vitamin D (calcitriol) instead of ergocalciferol.

hepatic_note: Requires hepatic 25-hydroxylation for activation. Severe liver disease may impair conversion — consider calcifediol or calcitriol.
contraindications: Malabsorption syndromes (relative — may need higher doses or alternative forms)

interactions.effect: CYP inducers increase vitamin D catabolism; may need higher doses

pediatric_specific_warnings: AAP recommends 400 IU/day for all infants from birth
Cholecalciferol (D3) preferred over ergocalciferol (D2) in many guidelines
Ensure adequate calcium intake concurrently
Do not exceed recommended doses without monitoring — risk of hypercalcaemia

administration.compatibility_note: Take with food containing fat for better absorption. Capsule can be punctured and liquid squeezed into mouth for younger children.

food_instructions: Take with food containing fat for better absorption.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Protect from light and moisture.
pregnancy_category: A (at recommended doses); D (at excessive doses)
lactation_note: Compatible with breastfeeding. Maternal supplementation increases breast milk vitamin D content.

therapeutic_use: Complicated skin and soft tissue infections
