unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=3 months. Once-daily dosing advantage over other carbapenems.

formulations.indian_brand: Invanz 1 g (MSD), Gerta 1 g (Zydus), Ertiga 1 g (Nutraferon)
formulations.indian_conc_note: 1 g lyophilised powder; reconstitute with NS for IV or lidocaine 1% for IM

dosing_bands.duration_note: 3 months–12 years: 15 mg/kg IV q12h (max 1 g/day); for complicated appendicitis with penicillin allergy
dosing_bands.notes: Dose frequency q12h for children 3 months–12 years (differs from adult once-daily dosing due to faster clearance). Max 1 g/day.

dosing_bands.duration_note: >12 years: 1 g IV once daily; for complicated appendicitis
dosing_bands.notes: Adult dose. Once-daily dosing advantage. Can give IM (with lidocaine diluent) for step-down.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Adults: 500 mg/day. No paediatric data in renal impairment.

hepatic_note: No hepatic dose adjustment.
contraindications: IM preparation with lidocaine: avoid IV administration

interactions.effect: Carbapenems drastically reduce valproic acid levels (by 60–100%); avoid concurrent use or use alternative anticonvulsant

monitoring_parameters: Signs of seizures (rare with ertapenem vs other carbapenems)
C. difficile monitoring if diarrhoea develops
pediatric_specific_warnings: q12h dosing in children <12 years (not once daily like adults)
Lower seizure risk than imipenem but still monitor
Does NOT cover Pseudomonas aeruginosa — not suitable for nosocomial infections
Generic Indian brands available (Gerta, Ertiga) making it more affordable

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute 1 g with 10 mL NS or water for injection
administration.dilution: Further dilute in 50 mL NS for infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: Infuse IV over 30 minutes
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS only — NOT D5W. Incompatible with solutions containing dextrose.
administration.storage: Store at 15–30°C. Reconstituted solution: use within 6h at room temperature or 24h refrigerated.

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute 1 g with 3.2 mL of 1% lidocaine (without epinephrine)
administration.compatibility_note: Deep IM into large muscle mass (gluteal). Do NOT give lidocaine-reconstituted solution IV.

food_instructions: Not applicable — injection.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C.
lactation_note: Present in breast milk at low levels. Compatible with breastfeeding.

unlicensed_note: Licensed for paediatric use at all ages. Multiple salt forms: estolate (best paediatric tolerability), stearate, ethylsuccinate.

formulations.indian_brand: Althrocin 125 mg/5 mL (Alembic), Eltocin 125 mg/5 mL (Ipca)
formulations.indian_conc_note: 125 mg/5 mL erythromycin estolate suspension; 60 mL bottle
100 mg/mL paediatric oral drops; 10 mL bottle with dropper
500 mg erythromycin lactobionate for IV infusion

dosing_bands.duration_note: 40–50 mg/kg/day PO/IV divided q6h (max 2 g/day) for 14 days; eradicates carrier state
dosing_bands.notes: Given concurrently with DAT for treatment of diphtheria. Also used for carrier eradication. Throat culture after completion to confirm clearance.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 40–50 mg/kg/day PO divided q6h (max 2 g/day) for 14 days; most effective in catarrhal stage
dosing_bands.notes: Alternative: azithromycin 10 mg/kg day 1 then 5 mg/kg days 2–5 (better compliance). Erythromycin estolate associated with pyloric stenosis in neonates <2 weeks — use azithromycin for neonatal pertussis.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 25–40 mg/kg/day PO divided QID for 14 days; limited evidence for faster resolution
dosing_bands.notes: Off-label for pityriasis rosea. Limited evidence base. May accelerate resolution if given early.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Primarily hepatically metabolised. No renal adjustment.

hepatic_note: Hepatotoxicity risk (especially estolate form). Avoid in pre-existing hepatic disease. Monitor LFTs if prolonged use.
contraindications: Concurrent use with ergotamine (risk of ergotism)
Concurrent use with terfenadine, astemizole, or cisapride (QT prolongation)

interactions.effect: Inhibits theophylline metabolism; increased theophylline toxicity; reduce dose by 25% and monitor levels
Inhibits carbamazepine metabolism; increased toxicity risk
Increases digoxin bioavailability by altering gut flora; monitor digoxin levels
May potentiate anticoagulant effect; monitor INR
Increased risk of rhabdomyolysis; avoid concurrent use

monitoring_parameters: Hepatic function (especially with estolate form or prolonged use)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis risk in infants <2 weeks receiving erythromycin — use azithromycin in neonates
Estolate salt has best taste/tolerability but highest hepatotoxicity risk
QT prolongation — avoid in patients on other QT-prolonging drugs
Multiple salt forms with different bioavailabilities — do not interchange without dose recalculation

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute suspension with water as directed on label. Shake well before each dose.
administration.compatibility_note: Stearate/base: give on empty stomach (1h before or 2h after food). Estolate/ethylsuccinate: can give with food.
administration.storage: Store tablets at 15–30°C. Reconstituted suspension: refrigerate; use within 14 days.

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute 500 mg with 10 mL sterile water
administration.dilution: Further dilute in 100–250 mL NS or D5W
administration.infusion_rate_note: Infuse over 20–60 minutes. Slow infusion reduces pain/phlebitis.
administration.compatibility_note: Highly irritant — use large vein or central line. Avoid IM (extremely painful).

food_instructions: Stearate/base forms: take on empty stomach. Estolate/ethylsuccinate: can take with food.
storage_instructions: Tablets: 15–30°C. Suspension: refrigerate after reconstitution; discard after 14 days.
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk at low levels. Compatible with breastfeeding. Theoretical risk of infantile pyloric stenosis.

unlicensed_note: Licensed for paediatric use at standard doses

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.05-0.1 mg PO daily for 4 months
dosing_bands.notes: Preterm neonates may need higher doses

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.1 mg PO daily for 4 months
0.15 mg/day (1-3 yr) up to 1 mg/day (>4 yr) PO for 4 months
dosing_bands.notes: Children 1-3 yr: 0.15 mg/day; >4 yr: up to 1 mg/day

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 mg PO daily for 4 months
1 mg PO daily to support erythropoiesis during active hemolysis; continue while hemolysis persists
dosing_bands.notes: Infants may use 0.1 mg/day

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 mg PO daily for chronic hemolysis (hereditary spherocytosis, elliptocytosis); long-term
1 mg PO daily; long-term supplementation to support erythropoiesis
1 mg PO daily; long-term supplementation to support erythropoiesis
dosing_bands.notes: Avoid in iron-overloaded patients already on chelation without confirmed folate deficiency

contraindications: Undiagnosed megaloblastic anaemia (may mask B12 deficiency)

interactions.effect: Folic acid may reduce phenytoin levels; monitor anticonvulsant efficacy
Folic acid may reduce methotrexate efficacy; use folinic acid for rescue instead
Sulfasalazine inhibits folate absorption; may need higher folic acid doses

monitoring_parameters: Serum vitamin B12 (to exclude deficiency before starting)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Always exclude vitamin B12 deficiency before initiating folic acid therapy to avoid masking subacute combined degeneration of the cord

administration.storage: Store at room temperature below 30°C; protect from light

food_instructions: Can be taken with or without food
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C; protect from light and moisture
lactation_note: Safe during breastfeeding; excreted in breast milk at adequate levels

unlicensed_note: Parenteral folic acid used when oral absorption is impaired; off-label in some settings

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 mg IM/IV/SC daily if oral absorption impaired; same duration as oral (4 months)
dosing_bands.notes: Switch to oral as soon as absorption is restored

interactions.effect: Folic acid may reduce phenytoin levels

pediatric_specific_warnings: Exclude B12 deficiency before treatment

administration.dilution: May be given undiluted IM/SC or diluted in NS/D5W for IV infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: Slow IV push over 1-2 minutes
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS and D5W
administration.storage: Store at room temperature; protect from light

storage_instructions: Store below 30°C; protect from light
lactation_note: Safe during breastfeeding

unlicensed_note: 5 mg dose used for antiepileptic drug-induced folate deficiency

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5 mg PO daily for 4 months for antiepileptic drug-induced deficiency
dosing_bands.notes: For phenytoin/phenobarbital/carbamazepine-induced folate deficiency

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5 mg PO daily for 4 months

interactions.effect: Folic acid may reduce phenytoin levels; monitor and adjust anticonvulsant dose

pediatric_specific_warnings: Monitor anticonvulsant levels closely when starting high-dose folic acid
food_instructions: Can be taken with or without food
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C; protect from light
lactation_note: Safe during breastfeeding

unlicensed_note: Used off-label for methanol poisoning; licensed for methotrexate rescue

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-2 mg/kg IV q4-6h (max 50 mg/dose) for methanol poisoning; enhances formate metabolism; continue until methanol <20 mg/dL
dosing_bands.notes: Enhances formate metabolism; use alongside fomepizole or ethanol and haemodialysis

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to leucovorin or folinic acid
Pernicious anaemia and B12-deficient megaloblastic anaemia (when used as sole therapy)

interactions.effect: Leucovorin rescues from methotrexate toxicity — this is a therapeutic interaction

pediatric_specific_warnings: Ensure adequate hydration; monitor for hypersensitivity reactions with IV administration

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute lyophilised powder with sterile water for injection
administration.dilution: Further dilute in NS or D5W for IV infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: Infuse over 15-30 minutes; max rate 160 mg/min
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS and D5W

food_instructions: Oral form: may be taken with or without food
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from light

administration.reconstitution: Already in solution
administration.dilution: Dilute to concentration <=0.09 mg/mL in 250-500 mL NS or D5W (for children dilute in 150-250 mL)
administration.infusion_rate_note: Infuse over minimum 4 hours; do NOT give as bolus or rapid infusion -- nephrotoxicity risk
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS and D5W; do not mix with calcium-containing solutions (Ringer's)
administration.storage: Store at 20-25 C; diluted solution stable 24 hours at room temperature

storage_instructions: Store at 20-25 C; protect from light
lactation_note: No data; bisphosphonates have very long skeletal half-life; avoid during lactation

unlicensed_note: Not FDA-labeled for pediatric OI; used off-label as alternative to pamidronate with fewer infusion days

formulations.indian_conc_note: 0.05 mg/mL; 100 mL infusion bag for osteoporosis dosing

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.025-0.05 mg/kg IV q6 months; single infusion day vs 3 days for pamidronate
dosing_bands.notes: 0.025-0.05 mg/kg IV single dose q6 months; alternative to pamidronate with fewer infusion days; monitor calcium 24-72h post-infusion; comparable BMD improvement

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Contraindicated if CrCl <35 mL/min; risk of renal deterioration

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to zoledronic acid or bisphosphonates
monitoring_parameters: Serum calcium, phosphate, magnesium at 24, 48, 72h post-infusion
Renal function before and after infusion
pediatric_specific_warnings: Acute phase reaction (fever, flu-like symptoms) very common with first dose -- premedicate with paracetamol
Hypocalcemia may be more severe than with pamidronate -- ensure calcium and vitamin D supplementation
Ensure adequate hydration before and during infusion
Renal function must be normal before each infusion

administration.dilution: Dilute 4 mg vial in 100 mL NS or D5W; for smaller children calculate appropriate volume
administration.infusion_rate_note: Infuse over minimum 30 minutes (some protocols 15 min); never less than 15 minutes
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS and D5W; do not mix with calcium-containing solutions
administration.storage: Store at 20-25 C; diluted solution stable 24 hours at 2-8 C

storage_instructions: Store at 20-25 C; protect from light
lactation_note: No data in lactation; very long skeletal half-life; avoid

therapeutic_use: Systemic inflammatory response syndrome of infectious origin with organ failure
Myasthenia gravis and other myoneural disorders
Immunodeficiency associated with other major defects
unlicensed_note: Licensed for primary immunodeficiency, ITP, Kawasaki disease; many other indications used off-label but evidence-based

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2 g/kg single infusion over 10-12 hours; within first 10 days of fever onset
dosing_bands.notes: 2 g/kg single infusion over 10-12h; within first 10 days of illness; concurrent aspirin; if fever persists >36h, second dose may be considered

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.4 g/kg/day IV x 5 days (total 2 g/kg); preferred in children over plasmapheresis
dosing_bands.notes: 0.4 g/kg/day x 5 days OR 1 g/kg/day x 2 days; preferred over plasma exchange in children

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.8-1 g/kg IV single dose for significant ITP bleeding; raises platelets within 24-48h
dosing_bands.notes: 0.8-1 g/kg single dose for acute ITP with significant bleeding; platelet rise within 24-48h

dosing_bands.duration_note: 400-600 mg/kg IV q3-4 weeks; maintain trough IgG >500 mg/dL; lifelong or until HSCT
dosing_bands.notes: 400-600 mg/kg IV q3-4wk; target trough IgG >500 mg/dL; bridge to HSCT; lifelong if no transplant

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5-1 g/kg IV over 2h for isoimmune hemolytic disease if TSB approaching exchange level
dosing_bands.notes: 0.5-1 g/kg IV over 2h for Rh/ABO hemolytic disease approaching exchange transfusion threshold; may repeat in 12h if needed

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2 g/kg IV divided over 2-5 days for autoimmune encephalitis/ADEM refractory to steroids
dosing_bands.notes: 2 g/kg total divided over 2-5 days; second-line after IV methylprednisolone for ADEM/autoimmune encephalitis

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Use sucrose-free product; infuse slowly; high osmolality IVIG can cause osmotic nephrosis

contraindications: IgA deficiency with anti-IgA antibodies (anaphylaxis risk)

interactions.effect: IVIG interferes with immune response; defer MMR/varicella vaccine 3-11 months after IVIG depending on dose

monitoring_parameters: Vital signs q15-30 min during infusion
IgA levels before first dose (if not known)
Renal function (BUN, Cr) before and after high-dose IVIG
Serum IgG trough levels for replacement therapy
Signs of hemolysis (Hb, LDH, haptoglobin) after high-dose use
black_box_warnings: Renal dysfunction and acute renal failure (especially with sucrose-containing products)
Thrombotic events may occur
pediatric_specific_warnings: IgA-deficient patients at risk of anaphylaxis -- screen before first infusion
Aseptic meningitis may occur 24-48h after high-dose IVIG -- reassure and manage symptomatically
Hemolytic anemia risk with high-dose IVIG (non-O blood groups) -- check Hb at 36-96h
Defer live vaccines (MMR, varicella) for 3-11 months depending on IVIG dose
Fluid overload risk in infants -- control infusion rate

administration.dilution: Use as supplied; do not dilute 10% products
administration.infusion_rate_note: Start at 0.5-1 mL/kg/hr for first 30 min; increase by 0.5-1 mL/kg/hr q15-30 min to max 4-8 mL/kg/hr as tolerated; for Kawasaki: complete 2 g/kg over 10-12 hours
administration.compatibility_note: Infuse via dedicated line with in-line filter; do not mix with other medications or fluids
administration.storage: Store at 2-8 C (check product-specific); do not freeze; some 10% products room temperature stable

storage_instructions: Most products: 2-8 C; check specific product; do not freeze; protect from light
lactation_note: IgG is a normal component of breast milk; safe during breastfeeding

unlicensed_note: Off-label for intrapleural fibrinolysis; evidence-based pediatric use for complicated parapneumonic effusion/empyema

dosing_bands.duration_note: 40,000 IU in 40 mL NS BID x 3 days via chest tube; dwell 4h then drain; for children >=10 kg
dosing_bands.notes: 40,000 IU in 40 mL NS instilled via chest tube BID x 3 days; clamp tube for 4h dwell time then drain; for children >=10 kg with complicated parapneumonic effusion/empyema

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10,000 IU in 10 mL NS BID x 3 days for infants <10 kg
dosing_bands.notes: 10,000 IU in 10 mL NS for children <10 kg; same dwell time protocol

contraindications: Recent (48h) major surgery or invasive procedure

interactions.effect: Increased bleeding risk; hold systemic anticoagulation during intrapleural therapy

monitoring_parameters: Chest tube output volume and character
pediatric_specific_warnings: Ensure chest tube functioning before instillation -- do not instill if tube blocked
Pain during instillation common -- premedicate with analgesic
Monitor for signs of systemic fibrinolysis (rare with intrapleural use)
If no improvement after 3 days, consider VATS

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute with NS to desired volume
administration.dilution: 40,000 IU in 40 mL NS (>=10 kg) or 10,000 IU in 10 mL NS (<10 kg)
administration.infusion_rate_note: Instill via chest tube; clamp tube for 4 hours; encourage patient repositioning during dwell time; then unclamp and drain
administration.compatibility_note: Use 0.9% NaCl only for dilution; instill at room temperature
administration.storage: Store powder at 2-8 C; reconstituted solution use within 24 hours

storage_instructions: Store powder at 2-8 C; reconstituted solution use within 24 hours
lactation_note: Intrapleural use has minimal systemic absorption; likely safe

unlicensed_note: Licensed for intrathecal use in children aged 4 years and older for severe spasticity of cerebral or spinal origin

formulations.indian_brand: Lioresal Intrathecal 0.05 mg/mL (imported via specialty distributors)

formulations.indian_conc_note: 500 mcg/mL for pump refill; 20 mL ampoule
2000 mcg/mL for pump refill; 5 mL and 20 mL ampoules

dosing_bands.duration_note: Screening test dose; if positive response, proceed to pump implantation. Maintenance: initial continuous infusion typically 25-200 mcg/day, titrated over weeks.
dosing_bands.notes: Test dose: 25 mcg intrathecal bolus via lumbar puncture; observe 4-8h for response. If inadequate, may give 50 mcg after 24h, then 75 mcg, then 100 mcg. Children aged >=4 years. Pump maintenance dose typically 100-800 mcg/day; some children require up to 1000 mcg/day.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Test dose for pump candidacy; maintenance via implanted pump with continuous intrathecal infusion
dosing_bands.notes: For severe refractory spasticity in hemiplegia; test dose 25-100 mcg intrathecal. Pump maintenance titrated to clinical response.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Screening test; if responsive, implant pump with continuous infusion; maintenance doses for dystonia may be higher than for spasticity alone
dosing_bands.notes: For severe dystonia-spasticity; test dose 25-100 mcg intrathecal. Maintenance pump doses for dystonia often 200-1000+ mcg/day. Children aged >=4 years.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal dose adjustment for intrathecal baclofen; negligible systemic absorption

hepatic_note: No hepatic dose adjustment required for intrathecal route

interactions.effect: Additive CNS depression; increased risk of respiratory depression and sedation

monitoring_parameters: Pump reservoir volume and refill schedule
Signs of baclofen withdrawal (hyperthermia, rigidity, seizures)
Signs of overdose (drowsiness, respiratory depression, coma)
black_box_warnings: Abrupt discontinuation of intrathecal baclofen can result in life-threatening withdrawal syndrome including high fever, altered mental status, exaggerated rebound spasticity, and rhabdomyolysis
pediatric_specific_warnings: Safety and efficacy not established below age 4 years
Children under 12 years may require lower daily doses
Pump malfunction, catheter kink or disconnection can cause sudden withdrawal — caregivers must be educated

administration.reconstitution: Not required; supplied as ready-to-use solution
administration.dilution: May be diluted with preservative-free 0.9% NaCl for test dose
administration.infusion_rate_note: Test dose: single bolus via lumbar puncture. Maintenance: continuous infusion via programmable intrathecal pump.
administration.compatibility_note: Use only preservative-free baclofen for intrathecal administration
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C. Do not freeze. Opened ampoules must be discarded after single use.

food_instructions: Not applicable (intrathecal route)
storage_instructions: Refrigerate at 2-8°C. Do not freeze. Protect from light.
lactation_note: Oral baclofen is excreted in breast milk; intrathecal systemic levels are low but caution advised

therapeutic_use: CNS prophylaxis/treatment in acute myeloid leukemia
unlicensed_note: Cytarabine injection is licensed for intrathecal use; age-adjusted dosing in children is standard practice per oncology protocols

formulations.indian_conc_note: 20 mg/mL; MUST use preservative-free vial for intrathecal use

dosing_bands.duration_note: Age-adjusted IT dose; frequency per protocol (typically every 1-4 weeks during chemotherapy cycles)
dosing_bands.notes: Age <1 year: 20 mg IT per dose. Use preservative-free cytarabine only. Given during each chemotherapy cycle per protocol.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Age-adjusted: 1-2 yr = 30 mg; 2-3 yr = 50 mg; per protocol schedule
dosing_bands.notes: Age 1-2 yr: 30 mg IT; Age 2-3 yr: 50 mg IT; Age >=3 yr: 70 mg IT. Frequency per chemotherapy protocol.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Standard adult dose for age >=3 years; frequency per protocol
dosing_bands.notes: Age >=3 yr: 70 mg IT per dose. Use preservative-free formulation only. Administer slowly over 1-5 minutes via lumbar puncture.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal adjustment for intrathecal cytarabine; systemic absorption is minimal

hepatic_note: No hepatic adjustment for intrathecal route

interactions.effect: Increased neurotoxicity risk when given together intrathecally; follow protocol spacing

monitoring_parameters: Neurological examination for signs of neurotoxicity
black_box_warnings: Must use preservative-free formulation for intrathecal administration
Chemical arachnoiditis and neurotoxicity may occur
pediatric_specific_warnings: Age-adjusted dosing is essential; do NOT use BSA-based dosing for intrathecal cytarabine
Monitor for arachnoiditis symptoms (headache, nausea, vomiting, fever)

administration.reconstitution: Use preservative-free 0.9% NaCl or Lactated Ringer for dilution if needed
administration.dilution: Dilute to a concentration of 1-5 mg/mL for IT use
administration.infusion_rate_note: Administer slowly over 1-5 minutes via lumbar puncture
administration.compatibility_note: ONLY preservative-free formulations are safe for intrathecal use
administration.storage: Store at room temperature 15-25°C. Use immediately after preparation.

food_instructions: Not applicable (intrathecal route)
storage_instructions: Store vials at 15-25°C. Protect from light.
lactation_note: Cytotoxic agent; contraindicated during breastfeeding

unlicensed_note: Licensed for pediatric use for intestinal amoebiasis

dosing_bands.duration_note: 30-40 mg/kg/day divided TID for 20 days
dosing_bands.notes: Luminal amebicide for asymptomatic cyst passers or after tissue amebicide therapy. Take with food. Total daily dose 30-40 mg/kg/day divided TID x20 days.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 650 mg TID for 20 days (adult dose)
dosing_bands.notes: Adult/adolescent dose: 650 mg PO TID x 20 days. Max 1.95 g/day.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal dose adjustment established; use with caution in renal impairment

hepatic_note: Contraindicated in hepatic impairment; contains iodine — may cause hepatotoxicity

interactions.effect: Iodoquinol contains iodine; may interfere with thyroid function tests for up to 6 months after treatment

pediatric_specific_warnings: Optic neuritis and optic atrophy reported with prolonged use
Do not exceed 20-day course or 1.95 g/day
food_instructions: Take with food to reduce GI upset. Tablets may be crushed and mixed with applesauce for children.
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C in a dry place. Protect from light.
lactation_note: Avoid during breastfeeding; iodine content may affect infant thyroid function

unlicensed_note: Licensed for acute bronchospasm in children; nebuliser solution used from birth in practice. MDI approved >=6 years.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 125 mcg/mL; 2 mL unit dose = 250 mcg; for younger children

dosing_bands.duration_note: 250 mcg nebulised q20min x3 doses in first hour with salbutamol; then q4-6h as needed
dosing_bands.notes: For children <20 kg or <5 years: 250 mcg per nebulisation. Combine with salbutamol in same nebuliser chamber. q20min x3 in first hour for acute episode.

dosing_bands.duration_note: q20min x3 doses in first hour with salbutamol; then q4-6h PRN
dosing_bands.notes: Children >=20 kg or >=5 years: 500 mcg per nebulisation. Combine with salbutamol. For severe acute bronchospasm, give q20min x3 in first hour.

dosing_bands.duration_note: q20min x3 in first hour; then q4-6h. Discontinue once acute phase resolves.
dosing_bands.notes: Add-on bronchodilator with continuous or repeated salbutamol nebs in status asthmaticus. Weight-based: <20 kg = 250 mcg; >=20 kg = 500 mcg.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 250-500 mcg nebulised q6-8h as add-on bronchodilator for acute episodes
dosing_bands.notes: For acute wheezing as add-on to salbutamol; q6-8h PRN.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal dose adjustment; minimal systemic absorption

hepatic_note: No hepatic dose adjustment required
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to ipratropium or atropine derivatives
Soya/peanut allergy (MDI formulation contains soya lecithin)

interactions.effect: Additive anticholinergic effects; increased risk of urinary retention, constipation, dry mouth

monitoring_parameters: Clinical response (oxygen saturation, respiratory rate, auscultation)
Heart rate (tachycardia with combined beta-agonist use)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Avoid contact with eyes during nebulisation — may cause acute angle-closure glaucoma
Use face mask with tight fit to avoid ocular exposure

administration.dilution: May be mixed with salbutamol respules in same nebuliser chamber
administration.infusion_rate_note: Drive nebuliser with oxygen at 6-8 L/min
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with salbutamol nebuliser solution
administration.storage: Store unit-dose vials at room temperature below 30°C. Protect from light.

food_instructions: Not applicable (inhaled route)
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C. Protect from light and moisture. Do not freeze.
lactation_note: Minimal systemic absorption; considered safe during lactation

drug_class: Iron (III) hydroxide polymaltose complex (non-ionic iron preparation)
unlicensed_note: Licensed for pediatric use including infants; well-tolerated oral iron preparation

formulations.indian_conc_note: 50 mg elemental iron/mL; 1 drop ≈ 2.5 mg elemental iron

formulations.indian_brand: Orofer Syrup (Emcure), Mumfer Syrup (Emcure), Feritin Syrup (Micro Labs)

formulations.indian_conc_note: 100 mg elemental iron per tablet; often combined with folic acid

dosing_bands.duration_note: 3-5 mg/kg/day elemental iron in 1-2 divided doses; continue for 3 months after Hb normalises
dosing_bands.notes: Use oral drops for infants. Give with or after meals for better GI tolerance. Iron polymaltose has fewer GI side effects than ferrous sulphate.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 3-5 mg/kg/day elemental iron in 1-2 doses with meals; continue 3 months after Hb normalises
dosing_bands.notes: Use syrup or chewable tablets. Better GI tolerance than ferrous sulphate. Can be taken with food without reducing absorption.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 3-5 mg/kg/day (max 200 mg elemental iron/day) in 1-2 doses
dosing_bands.notes: Use tablet formulation. Continue iron for 3 months after Hb normalises to replenish stores.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal adjustment required; oral iron not significantly affected by renal function

hepatic_note: Use with caution in hepatic disease; iron overload risk if co-existing hemochromatosis
contraindications: Non-iron-deficiency anemias (thalassemia major without iron deficiency)

interactions.effect: Iron polymaltose has less interaction than ionic iron salts, but separate dosing by 2 hours as a precaution
May reduce iron absorption; iron polymaltose less affected than ferrous salts

monitoring_parameters: Hemoglobin and hematocrit at baseline, 4 weeks, and 3 months
Serum ferritin to assess iron stores
Reticulocyte count at 1-2 weeks for early response
pediatric_specific_warnings: Keep out of reach of children — iron overdose is a leading cause of fatal poisoning in children under 6
May cause black stools — counsel parents this is expected

administration.dilution: Drops/syrup may be mixed with juice or water
administration.compatibility_note: Can be taken with food without significant reduction in absorption (advantage over ferrous salts)

food_instructions: Give with meals or immediately after meals. Can be mixed with fruit juice. Iron polymaltose can be taken with food without losing efficacy.
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C. Protect from light and moisture.
lactation_note: Iron supplementation is safe during lactation
reference_source: IAP Guidelines on Iron Deficiency Anemia

unlicensed_note: Licensed for pediatric TB treatment and prophylaxis; WHO-recommended doses for children are higher (10-15 mg/kg) than traditional adult-scaled doses

formulations.indian_conc_note: 50 mg/5 mL; often poorly tolerated (diarrhea) — crushed tablets preferred by some experts

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10-15 mg/kg/day PO once daily (max 300 mg); for 9-12 months total in miliary TB
dosing_bands.notes: Give with pyridoxine 1-2 mg/kg/day (max 25 mg). Part of RNTCP regimen. Give on empty stomach.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10-15 mg/kg/day PO daily for 12 months total for CNS TB
dosing_bands.notes: CNS TB requires 12-month therapy. Good CSF penetration. Always give with pyridoxine.

dosing_bands.indication: Respiratory tuberculosis, bacteriologically and histologically confirmed
dosing_bands.duration_note: 10-15 mg/kg/day PO daily (max 300 mg) as part of standard 6-month regimen; give with pyridoxine 1-2 mg/kg/day
dosing_bands.notes: Part of 2HRZE/4HR standard regimen. Daily dosing per Indian RNTCP/NTEP guidelines. Give on empty stomach 30 min before food.

dosing_bands.indication: Respiratory tuberculosis, not confirmed bacteriologically or histologically
dosing_bands.duration_note: Same dosing as confirmed respiratory TB; 6-month standard regimen
dosing_bands.notes: Clinically diagnosed TB. Same regimen as bacteriologically confirmed. Always give with pyridoxine.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 6-12 months depending on site; 10-15 mg/kg/day PO (max 300 mg)
dosing_bands.notes: Duration varies by site: bone/joint 9-12 months; lymph node 6 months; abdominal 6 months.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Reduce dose to 5 mg/kg/day or extend interval; risk of peripheral neuropathy increased

hepatic_note: Hepatotoxic — monitor LFTs. Withhold if ALT >5x ULN (asymptomatic) or >3x ULN with symptoms. Contraindicated in acute hepatic disease.

interactions.effect: INH inhibits phenytoin metabolism; increased phenytoin toxicity risk — monitor levels
INH inhibits carbamazepine metabolism; increased toxicity risk
INH induces CYP2E1; may increase hepatotoxic metabolite of paracetamol

monitoring_parameters: Baseline and monthly LFTs during first 3 months
Symptoms of hepatitis (nausea, vomiting, jaundice, dark urine)
Visual acuity if combined with ethambutol
black_box_warnings: Severe and sometimes fatal hepatitis may occur — risk increases with age, alcohol use, and concurrent hepatotoxic drugs
pediatric_specific_warnings: Hepatotoxicity risk is lower in children than adults but still present
Always co-administer pyridoxine (vitamin B6) 1-2 mg/kg/day to prevent peripheral neuropathy
Crushed tablets preferred over syrup (syrup causes diarrhea in many children)

administration.compatibility_note: Tablets may be crushed and mixed with a small amount of water or soft food
administration.storage: Store below 30°C. Protect from light and moisture.

food_instructions: Give on an empty stomach, 30 minutes before or 2 hours after food, for best absorption. If GI upset occurs, may give with food.
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C in a dry place. Protect from light.
lactation_note: Compatible with breastfeeding; small amounts in breast milk. Give pyridoxine to both mother and infant.
reference_source: WHO Guidance for National TB Programmes on Management of TB in Children
IAP Consensus Statement on TB in Children

therapeutic_use: Severe nodular/cystic acne refractory to conventional therapy
unlicensed_note: Licensed for severe recalcitrant nodular acne in patients >=12 years; off-label in younger children

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 0.5 mg/kg/day divided BID for first 4 weeks to minimise initial flare
dosing_bands.notes: Initial dose 0.5 mg/kg/day for first month, then increase to 1 mg/kg/day. Age >=12 years typically. Take with fatty food for best absorption.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5-1 mg/kg/day divided BID for 15-20 weeks total course; cumulative target 120-150 mg/kg
dosing_bands.notes: Maintenance dose after initial 4-week titration. Total course 15-20 weeks. Cumulative dose target 120-150 mg/kg. iPLEDGE/pregnancy prevention program required in females.

hepatic_note: Monitor LFTs at baseline and monthly. Discontinue if transaminases >3x ULN. May cause hypertriglyceridemia.
contraindications: Concurrent tetracycline use (risk of pseudotumor cerebri)

interactions.effect: Increased risk of pseudotumor cerebri (benign intracranial hypertension) — concomitant use contraindicated
Additive hypervitaminosis A toxicity — discontinue vitamin A supplements
Isotretinoin may reduce bone mineral density; phenytoin also affects bone — monitor

monitoring_parameters: Pregnancy test (monthly in females of childbearing potential)
Fasting lipid panel at baseline and monthly
Screen for depression and suicidal ideation
black_box_warnings: Must not be used by pregnant females — causes severe birth defects
Females of reproductive potential must use two forms of contraception
Reports of depression and suicidal ideation — monitor mood
pediatric_specific_warnings: Not recommended in children under 12 years unless under specialist care
May cause premature epiphyseal closure — monitor growth in prepubertal patients
Skeletal hyperostosis reported with prolonged use
Depression and behavioural changes — counsel families

administration.compatibility_note: Swallow capsules whole with a full glass of water; do not crush or chew
administration.storage: Store below 25°C. Protect from light and moisture.

food_instructions: Take with a fatty meal to improve absorption. Bioavailability is significantly increased with high-fat food.
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C. Protect from light. Do not freeze.
lactation_note: Contraindicated during breastfeeding; excreted in breast milk and highly teratogenic
reference_source: AAP Guidelines on Isotretinoin Use in Adolescents

unlicensed_note: Not formally licensed in children; however, widely used off-label in pediatric dermatophytosis. Safety and efficacy data limited in children <2 years.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 100 mg capsule; take with food for best absorption
10 mg/mL oral solution; take on empty stomach; better bioavailability than capsules

dosing_bands.duration_note: 3-5 mg/kg/day PO once daily for 2-4 weeks for resistant dermatophytosis; 6-12 weeks for onychomycosis
dosing_bands.notes: Capsules must be taken with a full meal. Oral solution should be taken on empty stomach. Children may need up to 6.2 mg/kg/day to achieve therapeutic levels.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 3-5 mg/kg/day PO for 5-7 days for pityriasis versicolor
dosing_bands.notes: For recurrent or widespread pityriasis versicolor. Short course 5-7 days.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Avoid oral solution (contains hydroxypropyl-beta-cyclodextrin which accumulates). Capsules may be used cautiously.

hepatic_note: Hepatotoxic — monitor LFTs. Contraindicated in active liver disease. Rare fulminant hepatitis reported.
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to itraconazole or other azoles
Co-administration with CYP3A4 substrates (simvastatin, lovastatin, ergot alkaloids, cisapride, pimozide, quinidine)
Heart failure or history of CHF

interactions.effect: Contraindicated — rhabdomyolysis risk due to CYP3A4 inhibition
Potent CYP3A4 inducer; reduces itraconazole levels below therapeutic range — avoid combination

monitoring_parameters: LFTs at baseline and if treatment exceeds 1 month
Itraconazole trough levels if response inadequate (target >0.5 mcg/mL)
black_box_warnings: Do not administer for treatment of onychomycosis in patients with evidence of ventricular dysfunction such as CHF
pediatric_specific_warnings: Safety and efficacy not formally established in children
Children <12 yr may require higher mg/kg doses (up to 6.2 mg/kg/day) to achieve therapeutic levels
Monitor growth and LFTs in prolonged courses

administration.compatibility_note: Capsules: take with full meal. Oral solution: take on empty stomach. Do NOT interchange capsules and solution mg-for-mg.

food_instructions: Capsules: take immediately after a full meal for maximum absorption. Oral solution: take on empty stomach for best absorption.
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C. Protect from light and moisture. Do not freeze oral solution.
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk; avoid during breastfeeding

therapeutic_use: Cystic fibrosis with CFTR gating mutations responsive to ivacaftor
unlicensed_note: FDA-approved for CF patients aged >=1 month with eligible CFTR mutations. Not widely available in India; accessed via specialty import.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 25 mg packet for infants/young children <14 kg
75 mg packet for children >=14 kg to <25 kg

dosing_bands.duration_note: Ongoing; 1 month to <6 years: 25 mg BID (<5 kg), 50 mg BID (5-<7 kg), 75 mg BID (7-<14 kg); take with fat-containing food
dosing_bands.notes: Weight-based granule dosing for 1 month to <6 yr. Mix granules with 5 mL soft food or liquid. Take with fat-containing food.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Ongoing; 150 mg PO q12h with fat-containing food for >=6 years
dosing_bands.notes: Age >=6 years (>=25 kg): 150 mg tablet PO q12h with fat-containing food. Lifelong therapy.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal adjustment; however, caution in severe renal impairment (limited data)

hepatic_note: Moderate hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh B): reduce to 150 mg once daily. Severe (Child-Pugh C): 150 mg once daily or less. Monitor LFTs quarterly.
contraindications: Concomitant strong CYP3A4 inducers (rifampicin, phenobarbital, carbamazepine, St John's wort)

interactions.effect: Markedly increase ivacaftor levels; reduce ivacaftor dose to 150 mg twice weekly if co-administered
Substantially decrease ivacaftor levels; co-administration not recommended

monitoring_parameters: LFTs at baseline, every 3 months for first year, then annually
Ophthalmologic exam in pediatric patients (cataracts reported)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Non-congenital lens opacities/cataracts reported in pediatric patients — baseline and follow-up ophthalmologic exams recommended
LFT elevations more common in pediatric patients

administration.dilution: Granules: mix contents of packet with 5 mL of soft food (applesauce) or liquid
administration.compatibility_note: Must be taken with fat-containing food (eggs, butter, peanut butter, cheese)

food_instructions: MUST take with fat-containing food (e.g., eggs, butter, cheese, whole milk). Absorption is significantly reduced without fat.
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C. Protect from moisture.
lactation_note: Unknown if excreted in breast milk; weigh benefits vs risks

unlicensed_note: Licensed for treatment of influenza in children >=7 years; prophylaxis in children >=5 years. Not recommended <5 years (treatment) or <7 years (prophylaxis in some guidelines).

formulations.indian_conc_note: 5 mg zanamivir + 20 mg lactose per blister; 10 mg dose = 2 inhalations; supplied with Rotahaler device

dosing_bands.indication: Influenza due to identified influenza virus
dosing_bands.duration_note: 10 mg (2 inhalations) BID x 5 days; start within 48 hrs of symptom onset. Age >=7 years.
dosing_bands.notes: Each dose = 2 inhalations of 5 mg. On day 1 if doses not 12 hrs apart, allow at least 2 hrs between doses. Age >=7 yrs for treatment.

dosing_bands.indication: Influenza due to identified influenza virus
dosing_bands.duration_note: 10 mg (2 inhalations) BID x 5 days; initiate within 48 hrs of onset
dosing_bands.notes: Same dose as adults. If using inhaled bronchodilator concurrently, administer bronchodilator first.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10 mg (2 inhalations) BID x 5 days; for suspected influenza in >=7 yrs
dosing_bands.notes: Use for clinically suspected influenza even if virus not yet identified. Start within 48 hrs of symptom onset.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10 mg (2 inhalations) BID x 5 days
dosing_bands.notes: Same as adult dosing. Initiate within 48 hrs of symptom onset.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: No renal dose adjustment required; zanamivir is minimally absorbed systemically (<20% bioavailability) following inhalation.

hepatic_note: No hepatic dose adjustment required; minimal systemic absorption.
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to zanamivir or lactose excipient (contains milk proteins)
Milk protein allergy (each blister contains 20 mg lactose with milk proteins)
Not recommended for treatment of influenza in children <7 years
Not recommended for prophylaxis in children <5 years

interactions.effect: Zanamivir may inhibit LAIV replication; do not administer LAIV until 2 weeks after zanamivir; do not give zanamivir within 48 hrs before LAIV or for 2 weeks after LAIV

monitoring_parameters: Respiratory function — particularly in patients with asthma or COPD
Signs of bronchospasm (wheeze, dyspnoea) during or after inhalation
Neuropsychiatric events (rare — delirium, hallucinations reported post-marketing)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Not recommended in children <7 years for treatment; <5 years for prophylaxis
Risk of bronchospasm — serious, sometimes fatal cases reported; NOT recommended in children with underlying airways disease (asthma, reactive airway disease)
Ensure child can use the Diskhaler/Rotahaler device properly before prescribing — requires coordinated inhalation
Contains lactose with milk proteins — contraindicated in milk protein allergy
Does NOT replace annual influenza vaccination
Post-marketing reports of neuropsychiatric events (hallucinations, delirium) — monitor closely

administration.compatibility_note: If using inhaled bronchodilator (e.g. salbutamol), administer the bronchodilator BEFORE zanamivir. Do not use via nebuliser — powder may clog device.
administration.storage: Store at 25°C (range 15–30°C). Do not puncture blister until ready to use in Diskhaler/Rotahaler device.

food_instructions: Not applicable — administered by oral inhalation, not oral ingestion.
storage_instructions: Store at 15–30°C. Keep in original packaging. Do not puncture blister until ready for use.
lactation_note: Unknown whether excreted in breast milk; use only if benefit outweighs risk.

unlicensed_note: OTC product; safe for use from birth. No specific age restriction.

formulations.indian_conc_note: 15% w/w zinc oxide in soft paraffin base; available 25 g, 50 g tubes
40% zinc oxide paste; available via import/online retailers in India
15.25% zinc oxide + lanolin + benzyl alcohol; available in India in 60 g, 125 g, 250 g tubs
Zinc oxide + calamine + clioquinol combination ointment

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply with every diaper change as needed; continue until rash resolves and for prevention
dosing_bands.notes: Apply a thick layer to clean, dry skin at every diaper change. First-line barrier protection. Gently clean area with warm water before reapplication; do not rub off residual layer.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply with every diaper change; continue as long as child is in diapers for prevention
dosing_bands.notes: Apply thick layer to affected area at each diaper change. Can be used for both prevention and treatment. If no improvement in 7 days, consider candidal superinfection.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply as needed to affected area with each diaper change or PRN for skin irritation
dosing_bands.notes: For older toddlers still in diapers. Same application technique — thick barrier layer.

hepatic_note: Not applicable — topical product with negligible systemic absorption.
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to zinc oxide or any excipients
Do not apply to infected or weeping skin (treat infection first)
For external use only — do not apply to eyes or mucous membranes
monitoring_parameters: Clinical improvement of rash within 48–72 hours
Monitor for signs of secondary candidal or bacterial infection (satellite lesions, pustules, worsening despite barrier care)
pediatric_specific_warnings: For external use only — keep out of reach of children for ingestion
If rash worsens or does not improve within 7 days, re-evaluate for candidal or bacterial superinfection
Avoid use with occlusive dressings unless directed
Some combination products contain fragrances — check for contact sensitisation

administration.compatibility_note: Clean and dry skin before application. Apply thick layer. Do not rub in completely — leave as barrier. Can be layered over antifungal cream if candidal co-infection.
administration.storage: Store at room temperature 15–30°C. Keep container tightly closed.

food_instructions: Not applicable — topical use only.
storage_instructions: Store at room temperature 15–30°C. Keep out of reach of children.
lactation_note: Safe in lactation; topical zinc oxide has negligible systemic absorption.

unlicensed_note: OTC medicated shampoo. FDA monograph approved for ZPT 0.3–2%. Paediatric use: physician to determine dose and appropriateness in young children.

formulations.indian_brand: Head & Shoulders (P&G India), Scalpe Plus (Glenmark — ketoconazole 2% + ZPT 1%)
formulations.indian_conc_note: 1% zinc pyrithione w/v; bottles typically 75 mL, 100 mL, 200 mL
Same formulation used as body wash for tinea versicolor / pityriasis versicolor

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply to wet scalp, lather, leave 3–5 minutes, rinse. Use 2–3 times per week. Continue maintenance 1–2x/week once controlled.
dosing_bands.notes: Mild OTC antifungal option. Apply, lather, rinse, reapply and leave 3–5 min before final rinse. Use in children >2 years or as directed by physician.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply to wet scalp 2–3x/week; lather, leave 3–5 min, rinse. Maintenance 1–2x/week.
dosing_bands.notes: Same as adult use. If inadequate response, consider ketoconazole 2% shampoo or combination with ZPT (e.g. Scalpe Plus).

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply as body wash to affected area, leave 5 minutes, rinse. Use 2–3 times per week for 2–4 weeks.
dosing_bands.notes: OTC maintenance option for pityriasis versicolor / tinea versicolor. Apply to wet skin, lather, leave 5 min, rinse.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply as body wash 2–3x/week, leave 5 min, rinse. OTC maintenance after initial antifungal treatment.
dosing_bands.notes: Same as adult. Effective maintenance treatment for recurrent tinea versicolor.

hepatic_note: Not applicable — topical product with negligible systemic absorption.
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to zinc pyrithione or any excipient
Avoid contact with eyes — may cause irritation
Do not use on broken or inflamed skin (open wounds)
monitoring_parameters: Clinical improvement in scaling and pruritus within 2–4 weeks
Monitor for contact dermatitis or worsening irritation
pediatric_specific_warnings: Use in children <2 years should be directed by a physician
Avoid contact with eyes; rinse thoroughly with water if contact occurs
If condition worsens or does not improve after 4 weeks of regular use, consider alternative diagnosis or treatment
Scalpe Plus (combination with ketoconazole) may be more potent — use with caution in young children

administration.compatibility_note: Wet hair/skin, apply shampoo, lather, leave 3–5 minutes, rinse, repeat. For body use, leave 5 min then rinse. Avoid eyes.
administration.storage: Store at room temperature. Keep bottle closed when not in use.

food_instructions: Not applicable — topical use only.
storage_instructions: Store at room temperature 15–30°C. Keep out of reach of children.
lactation_note: Topical use with negligible systemic absorption; considered safe during lactation.
reference_source: FDA OTC Monograph for Dandruff/Seborrheic Dermatitis

therapeutic_use: Adjunctive treatment of acute diarrhea in children
Reduction of duration and severity of diarrhea
unlicensed_note: WHO/UNICEF recommended for management of acute diarrhea in children <5 years. Included in India NLEM.

formulations.indian_brand: Zinconia DT 20 mg (Zuventus), Z&D 20 mg (Dr. Reddy's), BIBZinC 20 mg (BIBCOL), ZN 20 DT (Wallace)
formulations.indian_conc_note: 20 mg elemental zinc as zinc sulfate monohydrate; scored dispersible tablet; dissolve in 5–10 mL water or breast milk
10 mg elemental zinc dispersible tablet; for infants <6 months
20 mg elemental zinc per 5 mL; 100 mL bottle
Kit containing zinc sulfate 20 mg dispersible tablets + ORS sachets; for integrated diarrhea management

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5 mg/day (WHO 2024 reduced dose) or 10 mg/day (<6 months) for 10–14 days; give with ORS
dosing_bands.notes: For infants <6 months: 10 mg/day (half of 20 mg scored tablet dissolved in breast milk or water). WHO 2024 suggests 5 mg/day may be equally effective. Give for 10–14 days even after diarrhea stops.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 20 mg/day PO for 10–14 days; give alongside ORS
dosing_bands.notes: For children >6 months to 5 years: 20 mg (1 dispersible tablet or 5 mL syrup) once daily for 10–14 days. Continue full course even after diarrhea resolves. Reduces severity, duration, and recurrence for 2–3 months.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10 mg/day PO for <6 months for 10–14 days
dosing_bands.notes: Half tablet (10 mg) dissolved in breast milk or 5–10 mL water. Adjunctive to ORS and continued feeding.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 20 mg/day PO for >6 months for 10–14 days
dosing_bands.notes: 1 dispersible tablet (20 mg) dissolved in water once daily. Continue 10–14 days regardless of diarrhea resolution.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10 mg/day PO x 10–14 days for infants <6 months
dosing_bands.notes: Adjunct to ORS for rotavirus and other viral diarrhea. Continue full 10–14 day course.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 20 mg/day PO x 10–14 days for children >6 months
dosing_bands.notes: 20 mg elemental zinc once daily. Dissolve dispersible tablet in 5–10 mL water immediately before administration.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10 mg/day PO x 10–14 days per WHO guidelines for <6 months
dosing_bands.notes: Per WHO/UNICEF guidelines: 10 mg/day for infants <6 months. Give with ORS. Continue breastfeeding.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 20 mg/day PO x 10–14 days per WHO guidelines for >6 months to 5 years
dosing_bands.notes: Per WHO/UNICEF: 20 mg/day for children 6 months to 5 years. Adjunct to ORS. Reduces diarrhea duration by ~25% and stool output by ~30%. Prevents recurrence for 2–3 months.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Zinc accumulation possible in severe renal impairment; use with caution. Short 10–14 day course unlikely to cause toxicity at standard paediatric doses.

hepatic_note: No specific hepatic dose adjustment. Use with caution in severe hepatic impairment as zinc is hepatically metabolised.
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to zinc sulfate or any excipient
Do not use as sole treatment for dehydration — must be given with ORS

interactions.effect: Zinc chelates tetracyclines and quinolones reducing their absorption; separate administration by at least 2 hours
Zinc reduces penicillamine absorption; separate by at least 2 hours
Concurrent zinc and iron may reduce absorption of both; separate doses by 2 hours if co-administered
High-dose zinc can inhibit copper absorption; not clinically significant at standard paediatric diarrhea doses for 10–14 days

monitoring_parameters: Resolution of diarrhea (frequency and consistency of stools)
Tolerance of oral zinc (vomiting is common adverse effect — give after meals to reduce nausea)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Vomiting may occur — administer after meals or with food to reduce GI side effects
If child vomits within 30 minutes of dose, wait 10 minutes and re-dose
Must be given with ORS for diarrhea — zinc alone is NOT rehydration therapy
Continue breastfeeding during zinc and ORS treatment
Complete the full 10–14 day course even if diarrhea resolves earlier
For infants <6 months: dissolve half tablet in breast milk for easier administration
Taste may be unpleasant — mixing with breast milk or small volume of water helps acceptance

administration.reconstitution: Dissolve dispersible tablet in 5–10 mL of clean water or breast milk immediately before administration. Use teaspoon or medicine cup.
administration.compatibility_note: Can be given with ORS solution but preferably separately. Give after meals to reduce vomiting. Do not mix with iron supplements.
administration.storage: Store at room temperature below 30°C. Protect from moisture. Keep in original blister pack.

food_instructions: Give after meals to reduce nausea and vomiting. Can be dissolved in breast milk for young infants. Do not mix with tea, coffee, or iron-containing foods.
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C in a cool, dry place. Protect from moisture. Keep in original packaging.
lactation_note: Zinc supplementation is safe during lactation; zinc is a normal component of breast milk.
reference_source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Statement on Clinical Management of Acute Diarrhoea (2004, updated 2024)
Indian Academy of Pediatrics Guidelines on Zinc in Diarrhea
NEJM 2020 — Lower-Dose Zinc for Childhood Diarrhea Trial
India National Health Mission — ORS & Zinc Guidelines

unlicensed_note: Licensed for anemia of CKD in pediatrics; use in neonatal anemia is off-label but widely studied

formulations.indian_conc_note: Epoetin alfa 2000 IU/0.5 mL prefilled syringe
Epoetin alfa 4000 IU/1 mL prefilled syringe

dosing_bands.duration_note: 250-400 IU/kg SC 3 times per week with iron supplementation; limited benefit demonstrated, ROP risk debated
dosing_bands.notes: Start after 1 week of life; co-administer elemental iron 6 mg/kg/day; monitor reticulocyte count and Hb weekly

dosing_bands.duration_note: 250-400 IU/kg SC 3x/week; modest reduction in late transfusions; ROP concern remains
dosing_bands.notes: Supplement with iron; uncertain net benefit in neonatal anemia; discuss with parents

dosing_bands.duration_note: 50 U/kg SC/IV 3x/week for CKD anemia; target Hb 11-12 g/dL; supplement iron first; titrate dose every 4 weeks
dosing_bands.notes: Correct iron deficiency before starting; target ferritin >100 ng/mL and TSAT >20%; reduce dose if Hb rises >1 g/dL in 2 weeks

contraindications: Pure red cell aplasia (PRCA) following prior ESA therapy
Known hypersensitivity to mammalian cell-derived products

interactions.effect: May blunt erythropoietin response — monitor Hb closely
ESA-induced hypertension may require adjustment of antihypertensive therapy

monitoring_parameters: Hemoglobin/hematocrit weekly initially, then every 2-4 weeks
black_box_warnings: ESAs increase risk of death, MI, stroke, and tumor progression when targeting Hb >11 g/dL
Use lowest dose sufficient to reduce need for RBC transfusions
pediatric_specific_warnings: Monitor for hypertension in CKD patients
ROP risk in premature neonates — regular ophthalmic screening essential
Seizures reported with rapid Hb rise

administration.infusion_rate_note: IV: inject over 1-5 minutes; SC preferred for CKD patients not on dialysis
administration.compatibility_note: Do not mix with other drugs; do not shake vigorously
administration.storage: Refrigerate at 2-8°C; do not freeze; protect from light; single-use vials

food_instructions: Not applicable (injectable)
storage_instructions: Refrigerate at 2-8°C; do not freeze; protect from light
lactation_note: Unknown if excreted in human milk; use with caution

therapeutic_use: Emotional disorders with onset specific to childhood
unlicensed_note: FDA-approved for MDD in adolescents >=12 years; use for anxiety in children is off-label

dosing_bands.indication: Emotional disorders with onset specific to childhood
dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 5 mg/day; increase to 10 mg/day after 3 weeks if needed; max 20 mg/day; ages 12+; off-label for anxiety
dosing_bands.notes: Monitor for suicidal ideation especially first 4 weeks; taper gradually to discontinue; serotonin syndrome risk with MAOIs

hepatic_note: Max 10 mg/day in hepatic impairment
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to escitalopram or citalopram
Concurrent use of MAOIs or within 14 days of stopping MAOIs

interactions.effect: Risk of serotonin syndrome — contraindicated within 14 days of MAOI use
Increased risk of serotonin syndrome — use with caution
Increased bleeding risk — monitor for GI bleeding
Increased escitalopram levels — consider dose reduction

monitoring_parameters: Suicidal ideation — close monitoring in first 4 weeks
black_box_warnings: Increased risk of suicidal thinking and behavior in children and adolescents with MDD and other psychiatric disorders
pediatric_specific_warnings: FDA Black Box: suicidality risk in pediatric patients
Not approved <12 years
Monitor growth parameters on long-term use
Activation syndrome (agitation, insomnia) may occur initially
food_instructions: May be taken with or without food
storage_instructions: Store at room temperature 15-30°C; protect from moisture
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk; use only if benefits outweigh risks

unlicensed_note: Safety and efficacy not established in pediatric patients; used off-label in pediatric SVT and perioperative tachycardia

formulations.indian_conc_note: 10 mg/mL esmolol HCl concentrate for injection
10 mg/mL premixed infusion bag; double-strength 20 mg/mL also available

dosing_bands.duration_note: Loading: 100-500 mcg/kg IV over 1 min; Maintenance: 50-500 mcg/kg/min continuous infusion; titrate every 5-10 min
dosing_bands.notes: Ultra-short acting (t1/2 ~9 min); ideal for acute SVT/perioperative; continuous cardiac monitoring required; titrate to effect

contraindications: Heart block greater than first degree

Rebound hypertension if clonidine withdrawn — discontinue esmolol before tapering clonidine

monitoring_parameters: Blood pressure every 5 minutes during titration
IV site for extravasation (causes tissue necrosis)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Not FDA-approved for pediatric use
Risk of hypotension and bradycardia — continuous monitoring mandatory
Extravasation causes skin necrosis — use central line if possible

administration.dilution: May dilute 100 mg/10 mL vial; premixed bags ready to use
administration.infusion_rate_note: Bolus over 1 minute; maintenance via continuous infusion pump only
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with D5W, NS, D5LR; do not mix with sodium bicarbonate

food_instructions: Not applicable (injectable)
storage_instructions: Store at room temperature 25°C; do not freeze

unlicensed_note: FDA-approved for erosive esophagitis/GERD in children >=1 month; IV approved >=1 month

formulations.indian_conc_note: Enteric-coated granules 10 mg; mix with 15 mL water; for pediatric use
Lyophilized powder; reconstitute with 5 mL NS

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 mg/kg/day PO once daily for erosive esophagitis; 8-week course
dosing_bands.notes: Use granule sachets for infants; open capsule and sprinkle on applesauce if needed

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.7-1 mg/kg/day PO once daily; max 40 mg for erosive GERD
dosing_bands.notes: Reassess after 8 weeks; step-down to H2RA if possible; avoid long-term use >8 weeks without reassessment

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 mg/kg/day PO once daily (max 40 mg) for 8 weeks; alternative PPI

dosing_bands.duration_note: <20 kg: 10 mg IV daily; 20-55 kg: 20 mg IV daily; >55 kg: 40 mg IV daily; for upper GI bleed
dosing_bands.notes: IV for acute upper GI bleed; switch to oral as soon as tolerating feeds; continuous infusion may be considered for severe bleed

May increase methotrexate levels — consider temporary PPI withdrawal with high-dose MTX
Reduced absorption of azole antifungals due to increased gastric pH

monitoring_parameters: Magnesium level if long-term use (>1 year)
Bone density considerations with long-term use

Reassess need every 4-8 weeks; avoid unnecessary long-term use
Clostridium difficile risk with acid suppression

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute 40 mg vial with 5 mL NS
administration.dilution: Further dilute in 50 mL NS for infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: IV injection over minimum 3 minutes; infusion over 10-30 minutes
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS only; do not mix with other drugs
administration.storage: Reconstituted: use within 12 hours at room temperature

therapeutic_use: Other sex chromosome abnormalities, female phenotype, not elsewhere classified
unlicensed_note: Not specifically indicated for pediatric use; used off-label for puberty induction in hypogonadism/Turner syndrome under specialist guidance

formulations.indian_conc_note: Delivers 25 mcg estradiol per 24 hours; apply to lower abdomen

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start age 11-12 with ultra-low dose transdermal estradiol (1/8-1/4 of 25 mcg patch); titrate over 2-3 years to adult replacement; add progesterone once breakthrough bleeding occurs or after 2 years

dosing_bands.indication: Other sex chromosome abnormalities, female phenotype, not elsewhere classified

Oral estrogen reduces IGF-1 response to GH; transdermal route preferred
Estrogen increases TBG — may need increased levothyroxine dose

Cardiovascular and thromboembolic risk (primarily adult data)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Premature epiphyseal closure if excess dose — monitor bone age
Transdermal preferred over oral to minimize IGF-1 suppression and maximize GH effect
Specialist endocrine supervision mandatory

interactions.effect: Avoid live vaccines during treatment and for 3 months after stopping

Increased serious infection risk — do not combine

Signs of infection at every visit

Monitor growth and development
